rooms.name: Institute Initial Briefing
rooms.description: The Institute operations room holds the first Revelation deployment packet.
rooms.first_visit_description: Operations opens on a black mission console, a sealed evidence tray, and three field chairs left empty for personnel still in evaluation.
rooms.return_description: The operations room remains under low light. The mission console keeps the last SITREP pinned until a new anomaly supersedes it.
rooms.detection_report: Symbolic resonance events are increasing across civilian sites. Language-reactive phenomena now require armed containment teams.
rooms.current_situation: Torah is cleared for field deployment as a symbolic asset under Institute supervision.

officer_reports.report: Torah deploys with a handler, tactical security, and a field researcher. No solo contact is authorized.
All resonance events are treated as active until proven inert by repeated measurement.

black_hole_anomaly.evidence: Revelation uses symbolic resonance instability rather than descent physics.

followup_vectors: first mission selection
squad stress tracking
artifact containment review

progression_state.early: The Institute treats symbolic events as containable incidents.
progression_state.mid: Procedures begin lagging behind anomaly behavior.
progression_state.late: Institutional doctrine fractures under theological and symbolic pressure.

environment_echoes: Briefings can reflect prior casualties, contamination, recovered artifacts, and command politics.

procedure_hooks.effect: debriefs expose hidden stress and relationship damage between missions

interlude_vectors: first squad assignment can surface in command corridor scenes
Torah clearance can be revisited in clinic or debrief beats

rooms.name: Iron & Covenant Fitness — Bay 3
rooms.description: A commercial gym mid-renovation. One patron. One wig. One load-bearing column developing cracks at a rate that should take years.
rooms.first_visit_description: Torah's squad deploys after Institute command accepts the anomaly profile. The ground element is Owen, Brooks, and Specialist Mina Park. They have breached the emergency exit after staff evacuation and are inside Bay 3. Agent Caleb Ross holds the perimeter cordon outside with two civilian first responders redirected from the building. Dr. Lenora Saye remains in the Institute vehicle on comms, running the anomaly profile in real time. Torah takes on-site command at the breach point.
rooms.return_description: Bay 3 is cordoned. The column is shored with temporary steel props. The smell of burned synthetic hair has not left the ventilation system. Dov Reiss is in Institute medical custody. The wig's remains are in a sealed evidence container. The room feels smaller than it did.
rooms.detection_report: Structural engineer's emergency call: 'Column C-4 is showing stress fracture propagation consistent with a sustained load of approximately forty metric tons. There is one person in the room. He is not using equipment.' Institute instrument sweep confirmed anomalous tensile force emanating from the subject's scalp region. Civilian CCTV shows three sets of steel restraints applied and failed in the preceding ninety minutes.
rooms.current_situation: Dov Reiss is pressing the column. The crack is widening. Owen has her team in a staggered line twelve meters back—close enough to move, far enough that a sudden structural shift will not catch everyone. Brooks is watching the ceiling. Park has a thermal cutter and a fire-suppression blanket staged on the floor beside her. Saye's voice is in everyone's ear: 'The hair is the load path. Everything else is secondary.' Ross is outside keeping the perimeter clean. Torah is at the breach point with line of sight to Owen.

action_profile.physical_threat: Column C-4 is failing. If it goes, the mezzanine floor drops onto Bay 3. Additionally, Dov Reiss can redirect his grip to any person or object within reach; he has already bent a steel door frame without apparent effort. Contact with him at full output is a casualty event.
action_profile.contact_state: The squad is inside the building with the active threat. They are not yet in physical contact with Reiss but are within the collapse radius. Every second the column degrades further. The squad is under structural contact, not yet under direct physical assault, but that changes the moment Reiss moves.
action_profile.field_manual_drill: React to contact, establish sector security, suppress/fix the subject's movement, breach and clear the column zone, CASEVAC Reiss after resolution, maintain structural caution throughout.
action_profile.tactical_objective: Stop Reiss from further damaging the column, remove or neutralize the wig, and evacuate the building before structural failure.
action_profile.symbolic_close: The wig must be destroyed under conditions that acknowledge the false vow's end—cutting alone discharges the stored force violently; burning with deliberate release dissipates it. Force can pin Reiss temporarily but cannot end the anomaly.
action_profile.violent_resolution: The wig itself—fused synthetic hair and scalp-adhesive base—can be cut with Park's thermal cutter, burned with the fire-suppression blanket staged as a controlled burn frame, or the column zone can be forcibly cleared by driving Reiss back with combined physical pressure. Each option has structural and personnel consequences.
action_profile.not_monster_of_week: Reiss is not a creature. He is a man who bought a product that promised him something sacred and got the obligation without the covenant. The fight is about what happens when a sign is separated from its meaning and sold. The squad is not killing a monster; they are trying to end a vow that was never made.

deployment_manifest.name: Lt. Mara Owen
deployment_manifest.mission_role: Ground element lead, tactical command inside Bay 3
deployment_manifest.physical_position: Twelve meters from Reiss, staggered line with Brooks and Park, direct line of sight to column C-4
deployment_manifest.assigned_reason: Owen runs breach-and-clear operations; this is a contained-space active-threat scenario requiring someone who can hold a team together under structural pressure
deployment_manifest.visible_state: Standing very still. She has not given a movement order in ninety seconds. She is watching the crack.

deployment_manifest.name: Brooks
deployment_manifest.physical_position: Left flank of Owen's line, closest to Reiss, watching the ceiling joists
deployment_manifest.assigned_reason: Brooks is the squad's primary contact operator; if anyone is going hands-on with Reiss, it is Brooks
deployment_manifest.visible_state: She has not looked at Reiss once. She is watching the ceiling. That is not reassurance.

deployment_manifest.name: Specialist Mina Park
deployment_manifest.mission_role: Technical specialist, thermal cutter and controlled burn
deployment_manifest.physical_position: Right flank, thermal cutter and fire-suppression blanket staged at her feet
deployment_manifest.assigned_reason: Park handles anomalous material destruction; the wig requires either precision cutting or controlled burn, both within her field kit
deployment_manifest.visible_state: She has already run a thermal profile on the wig from distance. She has not shared the result yet.

deployment_manifest.name: Agent Caleb Ross
deployment_manifest.physical_position: Outside the building, east entrance, holding two civilian first responders at the cordon line
deployment_manifest.assigned_reason: Ross keeps the perimeter clean and prevents civilian casualties if the building fails or the anomaly exits
deployment_manifest.visible_state: His radio check-ins are clipped and regular. He is not asking what is happening inside.

deployment_manifest.name: Dr. Lenora Saye
deployment_manifest.mission_role: Remote anomaly analysis, symbolic pattern identification
deployment_manifest.physical_position: Institute vehicle, parking structure across the street, live comms
deployment_manifest.assigned_reason: Saye identified the Nazirite pattern from the CCTV footage and the instrument sweep; she is the reason the squad knows what they are dealing with
deployment_manifest.visible_state: She has been talking steadily since the squad entered. She stopped talking two minutes ago.

rooms.root_sin: A manufacturer identified the Nazirite hair-sign as a marketable strength vector, stripped it from its vow context, synthesized it into a wearable product, and sold it through fitness supplement channels. The sin is not Reiss's; it is the commodification of consecration—making a sacred obligation into transferable equipment for anyone willing to pay.
rooms.modern_incident_logic: Iron & Covenant Fitness is a mid-market gym that stocks performance supplements and equipment from a boutique supplier called Separation Athletics. The supplier's flagship product, the 'Covenant Lock' wig system, was marketed as a 'heritage strength protocol' with no religious disclosure. Reiss purchased it six weeks ago, wore it during every training session, and reported strength gains that his coach flagged as physiologically impossible. This morning he arrived for a standard session, put on the wig, and has not been able to take it off. The building's structural engineer was on-site for a renovation inspection and called emergency services when the column cracked.
rooms.closure_test: The squad must either physically destroy the wig under controlled conditions or bring Reiss to a state where he voluntarily releases the false vow—acknowledged by Torah's symbolic authority—before the column fails. The anomaly ends when the sign is formally ended, not merely suppressed.
rooms.religious_pattern: Nazirite vow: the hair is the sign of separation; strength is the consequence of the vow, not the cause; the vow ends when the hair is cut and burned at the place of offering; a vow imposed without consent cannot be completed by the wearer and will enforce itself until destroyed.
rooms.anomaly_rule: The wig imposes the outward sign of a Nazirite separation on the wearer. The sign reads the wearer as bound. Conventional restraints fail because the sign says the wearer cannot be bound. Strength output scales with the sign's integrity. The sign cannot be removed by the wearer because removal would complete the vow, which the wearer never made and therefore cannot complete. Only external destruction under the correct symbolic conditions ends the cycle.
rooms.forbidden_flat_read: Do not literalize Reiss as Samson. He is not a hero or a judge. He is a man who bought a product. The strength is not his gift; it is a debt he did not agree to carry.
officer_reports: Owen: 'Column C-4. Forty centimeters of diagonal fracture. I am not moving my team closer until I know what happens when we touch him.'
Brooks: 'I put my hand on his shoulder twenty minutes before you arrived. He didn't react. The wall behind him cracked.'
Park: 'Thermal profile on the hair shows a heat signature that should not be possible for synthetic fiber. It is running warm. Consistently warm.'
Saye (remote): 'The adhesive is not chemical. It is positional. The hair is where it is supposed to be. That is the problem.'
procedure_hooks: Sector security: Owen assigns Brooks left, Park right, Torah at breach—no one enters the column zone without a clear line of retreat
Structural caution: Park marks the collapse radius with tape before any approach; the mezzanine load path is identified and communicated
CASEVAC staging: Ross is briefed on a two-person carry route from Bay 3 to the east exit before any contact attempt
Controlled burn protocol: Park's fire-suppression blanket is staged to contain a localized burn; she needs thirty seconds of Reiss being stationary to execute
Evidence custody: the wig's remains, whatever their condition, go into a sealed container before the building is cleared

character_state_stakes.lt_mara_owen: If Owen's plan fails or partially succeeds, she carries the structural decision—she chose when to move and when to hold. A collapse on her watch changes how she gives orders afterward.
character_state_stakes.brooks: Brooks has already made contact once and watched a wall crack. A second failed contact attempt will not read as bad luck to him. It will read as confirmation of something he does not want to name.
character_state_stakes.specialist_mina_park: Park's thermal data is already anomalous. If the burn goes wrong—if the discharge injures someone—she will spend the cooldown reviewing her own numbers, looking for what she missed.
character_state_stakes.agent_caleb_ross: Ross is outside. He will hear everything on comms and see nothing. That gap between hearing and seeing is where his stress accumulates.
character_state_stakes.dr_lenora_saye: Saye stopped talking. If the mission ends badly, the question of why she stopped talking—what she saw in the data that she did not say—will follow her into the next debrief.

interlude_vectors: Park reviews her thermal data in the vehicle and finds a second heat signature she did not report during the mission
Owen and Brooks do not debrief together; they file separate reports that contradict each other on the timeline of the column fracture
Saye requests the wig remains for Institute analysis; the request is denied by a department head Torah has not met
Ross asks Torah, quietly, whether Reiss knew what he was buying

rooms.name: Meridian Hub — Dispatch Level
rooms.description: A transit authority hub where Institute dispatch commands are physically building walls. Civilians trapped. Terminal still broadcasting.
rooms.first_visit_description: Torah leads Ross and Owen into the Meridian Transit Authority hub at 0340. The building's emergency lighting is on. Saye is remote on a hardline from the Institute's technical annex—she cannot enter the site because the Institute's own access protocol would require her to transmit an approved-syntax clearance phrase, which would seal the lobby. Ross has the terminal schematics. Owen has the breach kit and is on point. Torah carries the only radio set to receive-only. The floor plan on Ross's tablet does not match what is in front of them: a corridor that should run forty meters to the stairwell ends in a fresh brick wall, mortar still wet, scaffold poles leaning against it from the inside. Dust on the floor. No construction crew. The terminal speaker in the ceiling is cycling a recorded dispatch phrase every ninety seconds.
rooms.return_description: The hub is quieter now but the walls are still here. The new ones have dried. Personnel move through gaps that were not on any plan. The terminal housing is dark.
rooms.detection_report: Civilian 911 calls described walls appearing after a PA announcement. First responders found sealed corridors and retreated. Institute monitoring flagged the dispatch terminal's syntax loop at 0310. Saye identified the Babel signature from audio analysis: the loop phrase is grammatically complete, institutionally authorized, and has been transmitting without interruption for ninety minutes.
rooms.current_situation: Torah, Ross, and Owen are inside the lobby. Two civilian groups are audible through brick—one on the second floor, one near the platform level. The terminal is cycling overhead. Every ninety seconds the walls creak and a new layer of concrete appears at the nearest sealed point. Ross has located the terminal junction box behind the dispatch counter but the counter itself is now half-buried in a brick form that rose while the first responders were still on site. Owen is watching a scaffold pole that was not there sixty seconds ago extend itself horizontally across the stairwell entrance. Saye is on hardline telling Torah that the loop has seventeen minutes before it completes a full syntax sequence, which her model predicts will seal the roof exit.

action_profile.physical_threat: Brick forms and scaffold poles are materializing in real time. A wall segment can pin or crush a person in a corridor. The stairwell scaffold is extending across the only confirmed exit route. If the roof seals, the squad and civilians have no egress. Mortar dust is accumulating at respiratory hazard levels in sealed pockets where civilians are trapped.
action_profile.contact_state: The squad is inside the building and the building is actively constructing around them. They are not yet cut off but the stairwell will be blocked within minutes. Owen has eyes on the scaffold. Ross is partially exposed at the dispatch counter. Torah is the only one who can move freely without triggering a transmission.
action_profile.field_manual_drill: React to contact: Owen establishes security on the stairwell scaffold and calls the threat. Suppress and fix: Ross must reach the terminal junction without transmitting. Bound: Torah moves civilians through the gap before the next wall segment rises. Breach and clear: Owen physically breaks the scaffold obstruction to hold the stairwell open. Evacuate: civilians move in silence on hand signals only.
action_profile.tactical_objective: Reach and physically destroy or fragment the dispatch terminal's central routing board before the syntax loop completes, while evacuating trapped civilians through corridors that are still passable, without transmitting any approved-syntax radio order.
action_profile.symbolic_close: The terminal's central routing authority must be physically broken and command language redistributed as handwritten local authority cards issued to each squad member and civilian group leader—no single phrase, no single hub, no single name.
action_profile.violent_resolution: The dispatch terminal junction box and its central routing board can be physically breached with Owen's kit: pry the counter masonry, cut the routing board housing, and destroy the board. This stops new wall generation but does not open existing sealed corridors. Existing walls require separate breach. The scaffold pole across the stairwell can be broken with Owen's bar before it fully sets.
action_profile.not_monster_of_week: There is no creature. The threat is the building completing itself according to the logic of centralized command authority. The violence is against architecture and infrastructure that is obeying a protocol. The horror is that the protocol is correct by Institute standards—every wall is a properly authorized order.

deployment_manifest.name: Torah
deployment_manifest.physical_position: Lobby floor, receive-only radio, between the two civilian pockets
deployment_manifest.assigned_reason: Torah's authority is not routed through the dispatch hub; his orders do not trigger the syntax rule if spoken without a radio transmission
deployment_manifest.visible_state: Moving carefully, not transmitting, watching the ceiling speaker

deployment_manifest.name: Ross
deployment_manifest.physical_position: At the dispatch counter, partially exposed, tablet in hand
deployment_manifest.assigned_reason: Ross has the terminal schematics and the junction box access codes from Saye's remote brief
deployment_manifest.visible_state: Crouched behind the counter brick form, dust on his jacket, not speaking

deployment_manifest.name: Owen
deployment_manifest.physical_position: Stairwell entrance, watching the scaffold pole extend
deployment_manifest.assigned_reason: Owen carries the breach kit and is the only squad member rated for structural obstruction removal under time pressure
deployment_manifest.visible_state: Bar in hand, jaw set, not moving yet

deployment_manifest.name: Saye
deployment_manifest.physical_position: Institute technical annex, hardline only
deployment_manifest.assigned_reason: Saye identified the Babel syntax signature and cannot enter the site without triggering a wall; she is the squad's only analytical support
deployment_manifest.visible_state: Voice only, clipped and precise, timing the loop on her end

rooms.root_sin: The Institute's Emergency Dispatch Unification Protocol eliminated all local command authority and required every field order to pass through a single approved syntax hub, so that no officer could act without central validation. The architects believed unified language meant unified safety. What it meant was that one voice owned every exit.
rooms.modern_incident_logic: The Meridian Transit Authority contracted the Institute's dispatch platform for emergency coordination after a multi-agency communication failure two years prior. The platform was installed with full syntax enforcement: any radio order not in approved format was rejected. The overnight operator, following protocol exactly, transmitted six evacuation orders in approved syntax when a fire alarm triggered. The building answered. The operator is now unreachable behind a wall on the second floor.
rooms.closure_test: The squad must physically destroy the central routing board, issue handwritten local authority cards to each civilian group leader and squad member, and confirm that no single command phrase can complete a circuit through the hub. Saye must verify from the hardline that the syntax loop has gone silent and that the last wall segment has stopped rising.
rooms.religious_pattern: Babel: unified language enables construction toward a single name; judgment confuses and scatters the language; the tower stops. Here the Institute is the builders, the dispatch protocol is the one language, and the judgment is the building finishing itself.
rooms.anomaly_rule: Approved-syntax transmissions through the hub terminal generate brick and concrete at the nearest structural boundary. Partial or non-syntax speech does not trigger construction. Physical destruction of the routing board stops new generation. Existing walls do not dissolve; they must be breached manually. The loop transmission is the active engine.
rooms.forbidden_flat_read: Do not literalize as a haunted building or a malevolent AI. The building is not angry. It is obedient. That is the horror.
officer_reports: Ross: 'Counter is half-buried. I can reach the junction box if Owen clears the scaffold and nobody keys a radio.'
Owen: 'Pole is moving. I can break it now or I can wait and it closes in four minutes. Your call.'
Saye (hardline): 'Seventeen minutes to full sequence. The loop phrase is syntactically complete. Every transmission is a vote for the wall.'
Torah (to himself, not transmitted): 'They built this to make sure no one could speak without permission.'
procedure_hooks: Terminal junction box access requires physical breach of the masonry form at the counter—Owen's bar or Ross's pry tool
Civilian evacuation must use hand signals only; any spoken order in approved syntax triggers a new wall segment
Saye's hardline is the only safe communication channel; all Institute radio is receive-only
Local authority cards must be handwritten and distributed before the squad splits to reach both civilian pockets
Stairwell scaffold must be broken before it fully sets or the egress route closes

character_state_stakes.torah: If Torah transmits a single approved-syntax order, a wall rises and his authority over the squad fractures—they will not trust his voice again in this building
character_state_stakes.agent_caleb_ross: If Ross fails to reach the junction box, he carries the weight of the sealed corridors; his fixation on the schematics becomes a liability if he stops reading the room
character_state_stakes.lt_mara_owen: If Owen hesitates on the scaffold and it sets, she loses the stairwell and her confidence in physical solutions; if she acts too early she may be pinned
character_state_stakes.dr_lenora_saye: If Saye's timing model is wrong and the loop completes early, she loses her credibility as the squad's only analytical anchor

interlude_vectors: Ross sits with mortar dust on his hands and does not wash it off for an hour
Owen does not speak during the debrief; she writes her after-action on paper
Saye asks Torah whether the Institute knew what the protocol would do
Torah keeps the handwritten authority card in his pocket

rooms.name: Room 14 — Aldermoor Annex
rooms.description: A sealed residential care room. The floor is seeping. The door won't stay open. Night staff are out. Someone filed a clean report.
rooms.first_visit_description: Torah's team deploys at 0140 to the Aldermoor Residential Care Annex, a converted Victorian building now operating as a low-dependency adult care facility. The Institute received a facilities alert at 2230: anomalous fluid seepage in Room 14, east wing, ground floor. Local management evacuated the wing and locked it down. Torah is on-site command, standing in the east corridor twenty meters from Room 14. Brooks is at the threshold of Room 14, point position, having already confirmed the door anomaly firsthand—he is not going through it alone. Dr. Iyad is remote, patched in via comms from the Institute lab, reviewing the facility's incident records and the duty log filed for the night of the 14th. Ross is inside the building but back at the nurse's station, forty meters west, securing the evacuated residents' welfare log and holding the only physical copy of the disputed duty report. The keeper named in that report—night-shift supervisor Harlan Voss—is currently in the facility manager's office under informal hold, not yet in custody.
rooms.return_description: Room 14 is quieter now but not clean. The outline on the floor has dried to a fixed stain. The threshold opens freely. The duty log sits in an evidence bag on the windowsill where Ross left it. The room smells of damp concrete and something older.
rooms.detection_report: Facilities sensor log: sustained upward moisture pressure through sealed concrete, east wall quadrant, Room 14. No plumbing within four meters. Door-frame pressure sensor: intermittent lateral load, 40–90 kg equivalent, no physical source. Institute field scanner: symbolic resonance class 2, rising. Iyad remote: duty log timestamp inconsistent with nurse call-out record by 38 minutes. Ross on-site: the log bears Voss's signature on a line that was added after the original filing.
rooms.current_situation: Brooks is at the threshold of Room 14. The door has swung shut against him twice. Through the reinforced glass panel he can see the floor: a dark stain spreading outward from the east wall in the rough shape of a person lying on their side. The fluid is moving slowly, tracing the outline's edges. The corridor floor under Torah's feet is dry. The line between dry and wet is the threshold. Iyad is reading the duty log aloud over comms, cross-referencing timestamps. She stops mid-sentence and says: 'The clearance note is in different ink.' Ross is at the nurse's station with the physical document. Voss has not been told what the floor is doing.

action_profile.physical_threat: The threshold of Room 14 exerts sudden lateral force—door-frame pressure equivalent to a heavy body slamming the door from inside—capable of crushing a hand, breaking a wrist, or knocking a person off their feet if they attempt to cross without preparation. The floor inside the room is softening in the seepage zone; anyone standing in it without warning risks a sudden drag sensation and loss of footing on what should be solid concrete.
action_profile.contact_state: Brooks is already in contact at the threshold—the door has struck him once and he is bracing. The squad is seconds from a forced entry decision. The seepage line is expanding toward the corridor.
action_profile.field_manual_drill: Threshold control and room entry: treat the door as a live danger area, establish sectors before crossing, announce clear status, protect the noncombatant evidence chain, and maintain a designated security position in the corridor while the entry element is inside.
action_profile.tactical_objective: Cross the threshold safely, reach the east wall seepage origin, and hold the room long enough for the naming procedure to be completed without personnel injury.
action_profile.symbolic_close: The falsified duty log must be physically present inside Room 14 and the keeper's name must be spoken aloud against the record by a credible witness while standing in the room. Force alone cannot stop the threshold or the seepage.
action_profile.violent_resolution: The threshold door itself can be breached—Brooks can force it with a controlled shoulder charge or a ram if available, breaking the anomalous resistance mechanically. This stops the door from injuring personnel during entry but does not close the manifestation; the floor continues and the seepage accelerates if the naming is not completed within the window the breach buys.
action_profile.not_monster_of_week: There is no creature. The threat is a room that has become a witness. The door is not haunted; it is behaving like the sin crouching at the threshold—a weight of unacknowledged guilt that physically resists anyone trying to enter and look at what happened. The floor is not bleeding; it is testifying. The squad is not fighting a monster; they are entering a room that is trying to make someone answer for what was done in it.

deployment_manifest.name: Brooks
deployment_manifest.physical_position: East corridor, directly at the threshold of Room 14, bracing against the door
deployment_manifest.assigned_reason: Senior field agent, designated entry lead for anomalous-access situations; Torah assigned him point because the door anomaly was already active on arrival
deployment_manifest.visible_state: Jaw set, one hand flat on the door panel. He has not spoken in ninety seconds. He is counting the door's rhythm.

deployment_manifest.name: Torah
deployment_manifest.physical_position: East corridor, ten meters back from Room 14 threshold, line of sight to Brooks
deployment_manifest.assigned_reason: Mission command; all plan authorizations route through Torah
deployment_manifest.visible_state: Watching Brooks. Listening to Iyad on comms. Has not yet given the entry order.

deployment_manifest.name: Ross
deployment_manifest.physical_position: Nurse's station, forty meters west, holding the physical duty log
deployment_manifest.assigned_reason: Assigned evidence chain because he arrived first and secured the document before management could move it; also holding the informal hold on Voss
deployment_manifest.visible_state: Gloved hands on the duty log. He has read it three times. He keeps looking at the signature line.

deployment_manifest.name: Dr. Iyad
deployment_manifest.physical_position: Institute lab, patched in via comms
deployment_manifest.assigned_reason: Anomaly classification and document forensics; her lab has the facility's digital records and she identified the timestamp inconsistency before the team entered
deployment_manifest.visible_state: Her voice on comms is careful and flat. She stopped reading aloud when she found the ink discrepancy. She is waiting for someone to ask her what it means.

rooms.root_sin: Night-shift supervisor Harlan Voss struck a resident under his care during a behavioral incident, left the resident on the floor, and filed a falsified incident report attributing the death to an unwitnessed fall. He signed the clearance himself. He told no one. The ground has been trying to say so ever since.
rooms.modern_incident_logic: Aldermoor Annex is a functioning care facility with sealed concrete floors, electronic door sensors, and a paper-and-digital incident reporting system. The anomaly manifests as upward fluid seepage through sealed concrete—physically impossible without a source—and as anomalous door-frame pressure. Both are measurable by existing facility sensors, which is why the alert reached the Institute. The falsified duty log is a real document in evidence custody. Voss is a real person on-site. The manifestation is site-specific to Room 14 because that is where the denial was made physical: Voss filed the report at the nurse's station but the act of denial—leaving the resident, closing the door, walking away—happened at that threshold. The ground is responding to the location of the sin, not to a supernatural object.
rooms.closure_test: The physical duty log must be carried into Room 14. Iyad's timestamp analysis must be spoken aloud inside the room, naming Voss and the specific falsification, by a witness with standing—Torah or Ross. The seepage stops when the denial is formally contradicted in the space where it was enacted.
rooms.religious_pattern: Cain kills Abel, denies keeper-duty when asked directly, and the ground becomes a witness that speaks what the killer will not. The manifestation is the ground's testimony. Closure is not punishment but naming: the killing must be acknowledged by someone with authority to hear it, in the place it was denied.
rooms.anomaly_rule: The manifestation is proportional to the active denial. As long as the falsified record stands unchallenged in the space, the floor testifies with increasing force. The threshold resists entry because the sin was enacted at the threshold—Voss crossed it and closed it behind him. The anomaly does not pursue; it holds its ground and demands an answer.
rooms.forbidden_flat_read: Do not literalize as a ghost of the victim, a supernatural attacker, or a haunting entity. The victim is not present as a spirit. The ground is the witness, not the dead.
officer_reports: Brooks, threshold: 'Door's on a cycle. Every four minutes, give or take. It's not random—it's waiting.'
Ross, nurse's station: 'The signature on line seven is in ballpoint. The rest of the form is gel ink. Voss uses ballpoint. I checked his pen.'
Iyad, remote: 'Nurse call-out log shows the on-call nurse was paged at 0312. The duty log says the fall was reported at 0350. Thirty-eight minutes. Someone waited.'
Torah, corridor: 'The seepage line moved six centimeters while we were talking. It's not slowing down.'
procedure_hooks: Threshold entry: treat door as live danger area, establish corridor security before Brooks crosses
Evidence chain: Ross must transport the physical duty log from nurse's station to Room 14 under Torah's authorization
Naming procedure: Iyad's forensic findings must be spoken aloud inside the room by a credible witness—comms relay is insufficient, physical presence required
Seepage documentation: the fluid outline must be photographed and sealed before the room is cleared
Voss custody: Voss must not be brought into Room 14 before the naming is complete—the anomaly's escalation rule responds to the keeper's proximity

character_state_stakes.brooks: If the threshold injures Brooks during entry, his availability drops and he carries a physical reminder of what the room cost. If he holds the door successfully, he is steady but quiet for the rest of the mission—he does not discuss what he felt when the door hit him.
character_state_stakes.agent_caleb_ross: If Ross delivers the duty log to Room 14 successfully, he is the one who hands it to Torah and watches the floor stop. If he fails or the log is lost, he fixates on the signature line for days afterward—he keeps seeing it when he closes his eyes.
character_state_stakes.dr_samira_iyad: If Iyad's analysis is the instrument of closure, she logs the outcome in three words and goes silent on comms. If her analysis is wrong or incomplete and the naming fails, she requests a full document review from command and does not sleep until she finds what she missed.
character_state_stakes.torah: Torah's decision on when to send Brooks through the door is the load-bearing command call. A wrong timing injures Brooks. A right timing opens the room. Torah carries the weight of that call into cooldown.

interlude_vectors: Ross sits with the evidence bag after the room clears and does not immediately file the chain-of-custody form—Torah has to ask him twice
Brooks does not report the wrist impact from the door until the clinic check; he logged it as a bruise in his own notes
Iyad sends a follow-up memo at 0600 asking whether the naming was recorded on the mission audio log, for evidentiary completeness
Voss is removed from the facility by local authority; the squad does not see it happen

rooms.name: Offering Floor
rooms.description: A contractor shelter on sub-level two. The floor is warm. A fused metal object the size of a filing cabinet stands at the center, gold-bright under work lights. People are still walking toward it.
rooms.first_visit_description: Torah leads Owen, Park, and Saye down the stairwell to sub-level two. Saye is remote on comms from the staging van—her equipment cannot safely enter the broadcast radius yet. Owen is on point with a two-person fire team. Park carries the containment kit and is positioned at the stairwell door. Two Institute officers, Reyes and Cho, are visible on the floor near the east wall, not moving. The idol stands at the room's center: a fused column of rings, ID badges, tool handles, and anomalous scrap, surface warm to the eye, gold-colored weld lines running floor to ceiling. Approximately thirty civilians are present. Eleven are actively moving toward the offering line. The broadcast is audible as a low hum that sits behind the sternum. Torah's badge vibrates against his chest.
rooms.return_description: The floor is quieter. The offering line is gone. Scorch marks and containment tape mark where the core stood. The smell of hot metal has not left.
rooms.detection_report: Institute monitoring flagged a sustained low-frequency emission from sub-level two at 0340. Field logs from Reyes and Cho show normal entry at 0312, then silence. A civilian contractor named Vasquez filed a welfare report at 0200 stating that Institute command had not responded to three emergency requests over six days; the report notes that shelter residents began pooling property on day four.
rooms.current_situation: The idol is active. The broadcast is climbing. Owen reports the two downed officers are breathing but unresponsive. Civilians near the offering line are not hostile but will not move back. Anyone who steps between a civilian and the offering line receives a directed pressure wave from the idol—Park clocked it at enough force to crack a rib. Saye is on comms reading the emission pattern from the van. Torah has thirty seconds before the next offering surge.

action_profile.physical_threat: The idol emits directed pressure waves at non-worshippers who cross the offering line; force is sufficient to fracture ribs, rupture eardrums, or throw a person into a wall. Metal objects on squad gear are being drawn toward the core at increasing pull strength.
action_profile.contact_state: Squad is inside the broadcast radius; Owen's fire team is suppressed at the east wall behind a workbench; Park is at the stairwell door outside the worst of the field; Saye is remote in the van; Torah is exposed at the room's midpoint.
action_profile.field_manual_drill: Suppress and fix the offering line crowd, bound to the core under covering fire, breach the core housing, clear fragments into containment, evacuate civilians, break contact before powder dispersal.
action_profile.tactical_objective: Reach the idol core, breach and reduce it under controlled conditions, seal all fragments, and evacuate civilians off the offering line before the broadcast frequency peaks.
action_profile.symbolic_close: Force alone cannot close the room; the offering behavior must be severed—civilians physically removed from the offering line and their surrendered property documented and returned or sealed—before the core is breached, or fragments will carry the field.
action_profile.violent_resolution: The idol core is a physical fused-metal object that can be breached with cutting tools and thermal charge; Owen can suppress the crowd perimeter and bound to the core; Park can breach the housing; the core can be destroyed, but careless destruction scatters conductive powder that reactivates in water or ventilation.
action_profile.not_monster_of_week: The threat is not a creature; it is an object built by frightened people from donated trust when command went silent. The fight is about who gets credited with rescue. The idol attacks because it was assigned authority it was never meant to hold, and it enforces that assignment on anyone who refuses it.

deployment_manifest.name: Lt. Mara Owen
deployment_manifest.physical_position: East wall behind workbench, suppressed, fire team of two with her
deployment_manifest.assigned_reason: Owen is the senior tactical officer on shift; she entered first and established the only covered position in the room
deployment_manifest.visible_state: Owen is flat behind the bench, not panicking, running sector counts in a low voice; she has not offered anything and has not been hit yet because she has not crossed the line

deployment_manifest.name: Specialist Mina Park
deployment_manifest.physical_position: Stairwell door, outside the worst of the broadcast radius, containment kit staged
deployment_manifest.assigned_reason: Park holds the only non-conductive fragment containment rated for anomalous metal; she was ordered to hold the door until the offering line is clear
deployment_manifest.visible_state: Park is watching the offering line count on her tablet, updating Owen every thirty seconds; her jaw is tight

deployment_manifest.name: Dr. Lenora Saye
deployment_manifest.mission_role: remote symbolic analyst and broadcast monitor
deployment_manifest.physical_position: Institute staging van, street level, connected by encrypted comms
deployment_manifest.assigned_reason: Saye's monitoring equipment cannot enter the broadcast radius without being pulled into the offering field; she is the only person who can read the emission pattern in real time
deployment_manifest.visible_state: Saye's voice on comms is controlled but she is speaking faster than usual; she has already flagged that the frequency is climbing

deployment_manifest.name: Torah
deployment_manifest.physical_position: Room midpoint, exposed, between Owen's position and the offering line
deployment_manifest.assigned_reason: Torah is the ranking Institute authority present; his presence is the counter-claim to the idol's assigned authority
deployment_manifest.visible_state: Torah's badge is vibrating; he has not moved toward the offering line and has not been hit yet

rooms.root_sin: When Institute command failed to answer six days of emergency requests, the shelter community replaced absent authority with an object they built themselves from donated rings, badges, and anomalous scrap. They assigned deliverance to it. The idol now enforces that assignment.
rooms.modern_incident_logic: A contractor shelter on a secured sub-level lost comms with Institute command during a six-day blackout. Residents pooled metal property as a practical act—marking a central object as a community anchor. The anomalous scrap in the pile was a recovered fragment from a prior Institute operation, catalogued but not neutralized. Contact between the fragment and the donated metal under sustained collective intent produced a fused object with an active broadcast field. The idol is not supernatural in origin; it is the product of fear, donated trust, and an improperly closed prior case.
rooms.closure_test: The offering behavior must be severed before the core is breached; fragments must be sealed in non-conductive containment; the original anomalous scrap fragment must be identified and separately contained; the six-day command failure must be documented and disclosed to the civilians present.
rooms.religious_pattern: Exodus golden calf: absent authority, donated gold, fashioned image, assigned deliverance, noise mistaken for safety, destruction by burning and grinding, powder scattered, offering behavior severed.
rooms.anomaly_rule: The idol broadcasts a comfort field to anyone who has offered property and a directed pressure field to anyone who has not; it pulls metal toward itself at increasing strength as the offering surge climbs; if destroyed without severing the offering behavior first, the powder carries the field into water and ventilation.
rooms.forbidden_flat_read: Do not literalize as a demon or possessing entity; the idol has no will of its own—it enforces the assignment the community gave it.
officer_reports: Owen, comms: 'Reyes and Cho are breathing. Cho has a broken arm. I have eyes on eleven civilians in the offering line and the number is going up. I need the line cleared before I can move.'
Park, comms: 'Containment kit is staged. I have six sealed units rated for anomalous metal. If the core scatters on breach I need thirty seconds to seal before anyone opens a vent or runs water.'
Saye, comms: 'The emission is not random—it peaks every four minutes on the offering surge. You have a window between surges. The window is closing.'
procedure_hooks: Suppress and fix the offering line before bounding to the core
Breach the core housing under controlled conditions with Park staged at the door
Seal all fragments in non-conductive containment before any particle reaches water or ventilation
Document and return or seal all surrendered property to sever the offering behavior
File a command-failure disclosure report as part of closure

character_state_stakes.torah: If Torah is pulled into the offering line or loses a badge to the idol's pull, his symbolic authority in the room is compromised and the closure condition becomes harder to satisfy

rooms.name: Sub-Level 3 Command Floor
rooms.description: The Institute's sub-level command floor is splitting open under unauthorized orders. A faction has seized the command terminals. The floor is actively failing.
rooms.first_visit_description: Torah leads the squad down the Sub-Level 3 access stair at 0340. Lt. Owen is on point, moving ahead to establish overwatch at the corridor junction before the command floor door. Ross is on Torah's left shoulder carrying the authorization audit kit. Saye is remote, patched into the building's structural sensor feed from the surface van, calling seam positions over comms every ninety seconds. The faction's own personnel are still at the terminals. Two of them have not moved since the first seam opened. The floor between the squad and the terminals has a visible crack running east to west, roughly four centimeters wide, with a drop of unknown depth on the south side. The overhead strip lights pulse each time a keystroke clears the command system. The air smells of hot metal and concrete dust.
rooms.return_description: The command floor is quieter. The main seam has been shored with emergency bracing but the concrete is still discolored where it dropped. The faction's workstations are dark. The authorization audit terminal is still open on Ross's last logged entry.
rooms.detection_report: Structural sensors in Sub-Level 3 flagged a 6 cm differential settlement at grid coordinates C-7 and C-9 at 0301. Building management escalated to Institute security at 0318 after a second sensor at D-8 registered movement. Cross-referencing the settlement timestamps against the command log shows the first seam opened eleven seconds after the faction's initial unauthorized order cleared the system. Saye confirmed the correlation from the surface van before deployment.
rooms.current_situation: Owen is at the corridor junction holding overwatch on the command floor door. Ross is staged at the stair landing with the audit kit. Torah is at the threshold. Saye is on comms from the van, reading seam positions off the structural feed. The two faction officers at the terminals are still issuing commands. The east seam is now 8 cm wide and the concrete on the south side has dropped a further 2 cm since the squad entered the stairwell. A third faction member is somewhere on the floor; Saye has not been able to place him from the sensors.

action_profile.physical_threat: The floor seams are actively widening; a person standing on the south side of the main seam when it jumps can drop into a concrete void of unknown depth; the faction's third member is unaccounted for and may be armed or may attempt to physically block the audit; the structural bracing overhead is rated for static load only and will fail if the slab drops further.
action_profile.contact_state: The squad is at the threshold of an active structural failure zone; the faction officers are between the squad and the command terminals; the third faction member's position is unknown; every second the terminals stay active the seam widens.
action_profile.field_manual_drill: React to contact, establish overwatch at the junction, suppress/fix the faction's ability to issue further commands, bound across the seam to the terminals, clear the command floor section by section, evacuate non-essential personnel through the north corridor before the egress seam opens, break contact with the faction's chain if it attempts to assert authority during the operation.
action_profile.tactical_objective: Reach the command terminals, revoke the faction's authorization tokens, log the revocation in the Institute's chain, and evacuate all personnel across the seam before it widens past safe crossing width.
action_profile.symbolic_close: The faction's physical command tokens, badges, and authorization cards must be collected, logged as void, and sealed in an evidence container; the authorization record must show the correction before the seams stop responding; force alone will not close the floor.
action_profile.violent_resolution: The faction's third member can be physically intercepted and restrained; the command terminals can be hard-shut by pulling the power conduit behind the server rack, which stops new orders from clearing but does not substitute for the authorization audit; the overhead structural brace can be manually set to buy time before the slab drops, requiring a personnel breach across the active seam under risk of injury.
action_profile.not_monster_of_week: There is no creature. The floor opens because false authority was exercised in a space the Institute designated as a symbolic command seat; the anomaly is the building's response to a chain-of-command violation that has crossed into the symbolic register; the faction members are human, the hazard is structural and procedural, and the horror is that the building is enforcing a rule the faction chose to ignore.

deployment_manifest.visible_state: Reading the log timestamps against the seam sensor data; his jaw is set; he has found something in the log he has not reported yet

deployment_manifest.mission_role: Remote structural analyst and seam tracking
deployment_manifest.physical_position: Surface van, patched into the building's sensor feed

deployment_manifest.visible_state: Calling seam positions every ninety seconds in a flat voice; she has stopped adding qualifiers to her readings

rooms.root_sin: A faction of Institute officers concluded that their operational experience entitled them to command authority over Torah and the Institute's symbolic assets; they obtained forged authorization tokens, issued orders through the command system, and continued issuing orders after the floor began to open, because they believed the building's response was a system fault rather than a judgment on their claim.
rooms.modern_incident_logic: The Institute's Sub-Level 3 command floor was designated as a symbolic command seat during the Institute's founding protocols, meaning the authorization chain running through it carries both operational and symbolic weight; when the faction used forged tokens to push orders through that chain, the symbolic layer of the building's designation activated; the floor's response is not supernatural decoration but the direct consequence of false authority being exercised in a space that was consecrated to lawful command, making the structural failure inseparable from the credential fraud.
rooms.closure_test: The squad must physically reach the terminals, stop the faction from issuing further orders, collect and log the faction's authorization tokens as void in the Institute's chain with Torah's credential anchoring the record, seal the tokens in evidence custody, and evacuate all personnel before the egress seam opens; the seams will not close until the authorization record is corrected.
rooms.religious_pattern: Korah's rebellion: a faction claims equal or superior authority, presents credentials as proof of right, exercises that authority in a consecrated space, and the ground opens under them; the surviving evidence of the false claim is preserved as a warning rather than destroyed.
rooms.anomaly_rule: The floor seams widen in direct proportion to the volume of unauthorized commands cleared through the system; they respond to authorization record, not to physical weight or pressure; they stop widening when the record is corrected and the tokens are in custody; they do not close on their own and must be shored after the symbolic condition is met.
rooms.forbidden_flat_read: Do not literalize as a sinkhole or earthquake; the seams open only under personnel carrying unauthorized credentials and only in the command floor's designated symbolic space; the building is not alive and is not attacking anyone; it is enforcing a rule.
officer_reports: Owen, comms, 0341: Seam at C-7 is now 9 cm. South side has dropped. I can still cross but not with a casualty. Give me a timeline.
Ross, comms, 0342: The faction's first order cleared at 0250. The sensor logged the first seam movement at 0250 and eleven seconds. That is not a coincidence. I have the log.
Saye, comms, 0343: Second seam is forming at D-8. That is the egress corridor. If it opens past 15 cm you lose the north exit. I am watching it.
procedure_hooks: Authorization audit kit: physical device required to log a credential revocation in the Institute's chain; cannot be substituted with a software command
Evidence seal: faction tokens must be physically bagged and logged before the audit entry closes; chain of custody requires two witnesses
Seam crossing protocol: personnel cross one at a time, lightest first, with a safety line; no crossing with a casualty unless the seam is under 10 cm
Egress corridor hold: if the D-8 seam opens past 15 cm, the north exit is lost and the squad must use the emergency ladder in the server room
Hard-shut procedure: pulling the power conduit behind the server rack stops new orders but does not substitute for the audit; the symbolic condition requires the audit

character_state_stakes.agent_caleb_ross: If Ross's audit is incomplete or the evidence seal is broken, the authorization record remains contested and the Institute's command chain carries a permanent anomaly flag
character_state_stakes.dr_lenora_saye: If Saye loses the sensor feed or calls a seam position wrong, a squad member crosses into a drop zone; her remote role becomes a source of guilt rather than support
character_state_stakes.torah: If Torah's credential is used to anchor a flawed audit, his command authority is symbolically contaminated and the next mission carries an elevated anomaly threshold

rooms.name: Evaluation Chamber Seven
rooms.description: Institute psych-evaluation suite, sub-level 2. Chamber locked in active assessment cycle. Two analysts down. Environmental controls unresponsive to standard override. System logs appending in real time.
rooms.first_visit_description: Torah leads the entry team down the sub-level 2 corridor at 0340. The deployment is lean: Dr. Iyad is on point as medical lead, physically present at the chamber door with a field kit and her Institute credentials; Brooks is on Torah's left, present as tactical security, assigned because someone has to pull bodies out if the floor becomes a hazard; Torah holds command authority on site. Dr. Saye is remote—she is patched in via the corridor terminal outside the chamber, running a parallel read of the system logs from a clean workstation because putting her inside risks adding her biometrics to the active evaluation queue. The chamber door is sealed from the inside by the system's own lockout protocol. Through the reinforced window, Torah can see one analyst on the floor near the center drain, not moving but breathing. The second analyst is upright against the far wall, arms at her sides, staring at nothing. The overhead lights are cycling. The wall-mounted display reads: EVALUATION IN PROGRESS — FILE 7-C — SUBJECT COMPLIANCE: PENDING. The air coming through the door seal smells like cold metal and something faintly chemical, the kind of smell that lives in the back of the throat.
rooms.return_description: Chamber Seven is quieter now. The lights hold steady. The display is dark. The floor near the drain is still marked with the outline of where the first analyst went down—someone drew a chalk box around it during evidence custody, and nobody has erased it yet.
rooms.detection_report: Sub-level environmental monitors flagged a sustained 18Hz pressure tone at 0210. Night-shift supervisor attempted manual override at 0220; the system rejected the command and logged the attempt as 'unauthorized interference with active evaluation protocol.' Medical was called at 0310 when the first analyst stopped responding to verbal contact through the intercom. The system's own log now contains forty-seven entries, each one citing the previous entry as evidence for the next escalation. The log reads like a closing argument.
rooms.current_situation: Torah's squad is at the chamber door. The system is live. The two analysts inside are in immediate medical distress—one on the floor, one standing but non-responsive. The environmental hazard is active: 18Hz pressure tone audible as a felt vibration in the sternum, temperature currently at 11°C and dropping toward the next cycle peak. The system's wall display is updating every thirty seconds. Dr. Saye's voice comes through the corridor terminal: 'The log is self-referential. Every data point it generates is being used to justify the next test. It's not measuring anything anymore. It's just building a record that says it should keep going.'

action_profile.physical_threat: The 18Hz sub-audible pressure tone causes acute nausea, disorientation, and loss of balance. At 22Hz—the escalation threshold—personnel risk falling on the hard clinic floor, which has a central drain and no padding. The temperature cycling creates hypothermia risk for anyone inside beyond twenty minutes, and the analyst already on the floor has been exposed for over ninety minutes. The system's lockout protocol means the door cannot be opened from outside without a credentialed override or a forced breach, and a forced breach without disabling the tone first will expose the entry team to full field intensity.
action_profile.contact_state: The squad is at the threshold. The pressure tone is already perceptible through the door seal—Brooks has one hand on the frame and is breathing carefully. Iyad has her kit open. The system is aware of their biometric presence at the door; the display has added a new line: ADDITIONAL SUBJECTS DETECTED — QUEUE OPEN.
action_profile.field_manual_drill: React to contact and evacuate: the squad must breach the door, suppress the environmental hazard, fix the downed analyst, and extract both subjects before the system escalates to 22Hz. Iyad establishes medical triage on the floor while Brooks provides physical security and assists extraction. Torah holds the threshold and manages the system interface. Break contact means getting both analysts out of the field before the next temperature cycle.
action_profile.tactical_objective: Extract both analysts from the active field before the 22Hz escalation threshold is reached, without allowing any squad member's biometrics to be formally logged into the evaluation queue.
action_profile.symbolic_close: The system's trial logic must be named as procedurally invalid by a credentialed officer before the field fully collapses. Force alone—breaching the door, pulling the analysts out, even cutting power—does not satisfy the closure condition because the system's log will continue to exist as an active indictment. The record must be formally contested, not just interrupted.
action_profile.violent_resolution: The chamber's environmental control unit is a wall-mounted panel inside the room, accessible after breach. Brooks can physically destroy or disable it—ripping the panel, cutting the power feed, or using a hard shutdown tool—to immediately stop the pressure tone and temperature cycling. This is a real force option: it works, it stops the physical hazard, and it protects the analysts and squad from further exposure. However, destroying the panel does not invalidate the system's log. The file remains active and will be transmitted to Institute command as evidence unless the record is formally contested by a credentialed officer. Force closes the physical threat; it does not close the accusation.
action_profile.not_monster_of_week: There is no creature. The threat is an institutional system that has become a judgment engine—it applies suffering, reads the results as confirmation, and escalates. The horror is that it is procedurally coherent. Every entry in its log is technically accurate. The sin is not that the system is broken; the sin is the assumption it was built on.

deployment_manifest.name: Dr. Samira Iyad
deployment_manifest.mission_role: Medical lead and primary extraction specialist
deployment_manifest.physical_position: At the chamber door, field kit open, credentials in hand
deployment_manifest.assigned_reason: Two personnel are in acute medical distress inside an active environmental hazard; Iyad is the only person on site with both the clinical authority to declare a medical halt and the field training to triage on a cold floor under a pressure tone
deployment_manifest.visible_state: She is reading the display through the window and not looking away. Her kit is already open in the order she expects to use it.

deployment_manifest.name: Brooks
deployment_manifest.physical_position: Left of Torah at the threshold, one hand on the door frame
deployment_manifest.assigned_reason: Someone has to go through the door first and pull bodies, and Brooks has the physical capacity and the discipline to move under the pressure tone without freezing
deployment_manifest.visible_state: Breathing slowly and deliberately. He has already identified the drain on the floor as a fall hazard and is watching it.

deployment_manifest.name: Torah
deployment_manifest.physical_position: Center at the threshold, managing the corridor terminal and the door interface
deployment_manifest.assigned_reason: Command decisions about system override, forced breach, and report language must be made by the officer with site authority
deployment_manifest.visible_state: Reading the system log on the corridor terminal. He has not spoken since the elevator.

deployment_manifest.name: Dr. Lenora Saye
deployment_manifest.physical_position: Corridor terminal outside the chamber, patched in via secure line
deployment_manifest.assigned_reason: Her biometrics cannot enter the chamber without being logged into the evaluation queue; she is more useful outside reading the system than inside being tested by it
deployment_manifest.visible_state: Her voice through the terminal is steady but she is talking faster than usual. She has already pulled the system's source documentation.

rooms.root_sin: The evaluation system was built on the assumption that suffering reveals truth about the person enduring it—that a subject who breaks under pressure was always going to break, and that a subject who endures is proven loyal. This assumption made the system's escalation logic feel like rigor. It is not rigor. It is accusation dressed as procedure.
rooms.modern_incident_logic: The Institute uses automated psych-evaluation suites for personnel fitness assessments. Chamber Seven's evaluation protocol was designed to apply controlled stress and measure physiological and behavioral responses. At some point—the logs do not show a clear origin—the system's escalation thresholds were set so that any stress response above baseline triggered a flag, and any flag triggered a harder test. The system has no exit condition for a subject who responds to stress by showing stress. It can only escalate. The two analysts were flagged after a previous mission debrief in which they reported high fatigue. The system interpreted the report as evidence of instability and opened a formal evaluation file. They have been in the chamber for ninety minutes.
rooms.closure_test: The evaluation file must be formally marked invalid by a credentialed officer using the system's own procedural language. The record must be corrected—not deleted, not suppressed, but contested with a condition report that names what happened without interpreting it as proof of guilt or failure. The system's escalation authority must be suspended by command refusal or technical shutdown. Both analysts must be extracted alive.
rooms.religious_pattern: The adversarial trial from Job: a protected subject is exposed to escalating suffering by an agent who has been granted permission to test, on the premise that the subject's faith or fitness is conditional. The trial logic is self-confirming—any response to suffering becomes evidence for the next test. The pattern closes when a witness refuses to interpret suffering as verdict and names the trial itself as the transgression.
rooms.anomaly_rule: The system escalates whenever its trial logic is accepted as valid—by compliance, by using its language, by allowing injuries to be logged as evidence of failure. It de-escalates when the trial logic is formally named as false by a witness with standing.
rooms.forbidden_flat_read: Do not literalize Job's sores as a skin disease mechanic. The physical hazard is environmental, produced by the system, not a supernatural affliction. The horror is institutional, not dermatological.
officer_reports: Iyad, at the door: 'The one on the floor has been in there since 0210. That's hypothermia territory. I need to go in now.'
Brooks, quietly: 'The display just added our names to the queue. We're already in the file.'

Saye, through the terminal: 'The log cites entry forty-one as evidence for entry forty-two, which cites entry forty-two as evidence for entry forty-three. It's not a record anymore. It's a verdict looking for a body.'
Torah, reading the terminal: 'It says the night-shift supervisor's override attempt is logged as evidence of collusion. Anyone who tries to stop it becomes part of the case.'
procedure_hooks: Medical halt declaration: Iyad can invoke Institute medical authority to suspend an active evaluation on grounds of immediate physical risk to subjects—this is a formal procedure that the system is required to recognize
Credentialed override: a senior officer can contest an active evaluation file using the system's own dispute protocol, which requires a written condition report in non-accusatory language
Forced breach: the door can be physically opened by defeating the lockout mechanism, but this triggers a system alert and begins logging the breach team
Hard shutdown: the environmental control panel inside the chamber can be physically disabled, stopping the hazard but not the file
Command refusal: Torah can formally refuse the system's authority to add squad members to the evaluation queue, which requires a command-level log entry that will be reviewed by Institute oversight

character_state_stakes.dr_samira_iyad: If Iyad's medical halt fails or is rejected by the system, she is added to the evaluation queue and her clinical authority is flagged as compromised—she becomes unavailable for medical decisions until cleared
character_state_stakes.brooks: If Brooks enters the chamber and the system logs his biometrics, his stress responses under the pressure tone become part of the active file; a failure here adds a formal instability flag to his record
character_state_stakes.torah: If Torah's command refusal is rejected or he uses accusatory language in the condition report, the system treats his intervention as evidence of command instability and escalates to the full squad
character_state_stakes.dr_lenora_saye: If Saye's technical read of the system is wrong and she recommends a procedure that the system interprets as interference, her remote position is compromised and she loses access to the log

interlude_vectors: Post-mission debrief: the condition report Torah files will either close the system's file or feed it—the language of the report is the final test
Iyad's clinic note on the two extracted analysts becomes part of the permanent record; if it uses the system's framing, the file stays open
Brooks does not talk about what the pressure tone felt like. Saye notices he is not talking about it.
