rooms.name: Executive Floor, Voss Tower — Level 38
rooms.description: A sealed corporate floor. The director is already gone. The judgment is running.
rooms.first_visit_description: Torah's team arrives via service elevator to Level 38 of Voss Tower, a glass-and-steel executive floor locked down by building security forty minutes ago after a wellness call went unanswered. Lt. Owen leads the entry stack. Brooks holds the elevator and monitors the stairwell below. Dr. Saye is on comms from the Institute van in the parking structure, pulling Voss's public record and the broadcast transcript. Specialist Park is on Torah's left with a field kit and a portable signal jammer. The floor smells of cut grass and copper. Every screen on the ceiling grid is running the same looped video: Harlan Voss at a podium, two hours ago, telling a packed auditorium that his organization recognizes no authority above its own judgment. The loop is silent but the captions are live. Eleven staff members are visible through the glass partition. Two are seated on the floor. One is pressed against the far window, watching something below the sill. The corner office door is closed. Through the frosted glass panel beside it, a shape moves low and slow.
rooms.return_description: Level 38 is quieter now. The screens are dark or cycling Institute holding frames. The carpet near the planter boxes is torn. The smell of cut grass has not left.
rooms.detection_report: Building security flagged an executive wellness call at 09:14. A junior associate reported that Director Voss had not responded to knocks since 08:50, approximately twenty minutes after the all-staff broadcast concluded. Two staff members on the floor reported feeling 'very heavy' and 'unable to think of words' before security locked the floor. Institute monitoring flagged the broadcast transcript at 08:47 as a Category 2 self-sovereignty declaration. Deployment authorized at 09:31.
rooms.current_situation: Torah's stack is through the service door. Owen has eyes on the corner office. The shape behind the frosted glass is moving on all fours. One of the seated staff members on the floor has begun pulling at the carpet fibers with both hands, slowly, without looking up. The broadcast loop is audible now: a speaker somewhere is running audio. Voss's voice fills the corridor in a flat, even tone. The captions on the nearest screen read: WE ARE THE FINAL AUTHORITY ON WHAT WE ARE.

action_profile.physical_threat: Voss in full degradation is large, fast on all fours, and does not respond to verbal commands. He has broken the interior glass of his office once already. The two staff members showing early signs will physically resist removal if touched before the broadcast is interrupted. The ornamental planter boxes have been partially demolished; ceramic shards and exposed rebar are on the floor.
action_profile.contact_state: The squad is inside the floor. The threat is behind a closed door eight meters ahead. Two compromised civilians are between the stack and the office. The broadcast audio is live and escalating the effect in real time.
action_profile.field_manual_drill: React to contact on office breach, suppress and fix Voss to prevent injury to staff, clear the floor of compromised civilians via evacuation before full degradation spreads, establish cordon around the office, then execute symbolic close.
action_profile.tactical_objective: Breach the corner office, establish physical control of Voss without lethal force, evacuate compromised staff, and interrupt the broadcast before the seventh hour mark.
action_profile.symbolic_close: The declaration must be formally refuted or withdrawn in the same space. A written retraction read aloud by a witness, or a direct verbal acknowledgment from Voss if cognition partially returns, closes the judgment locally. Destroying the broadcast equipment without the refutation accelerates spread.
action_profile.violent_resolution: The broadcast server rack is in the IT closet adjacent to the corner office. It can be physically destroyed. Doing so silences the audio immediately and slows new degradation onset, but without the spoken refutation, Voss's condition does not reverse and the two compromised staff members do not recover. The rack destruction is a valid tactical step that buys time but does not close the case.
action_profile.not_monster_of_week: This is not a creature. It is a man under a specific judgment for a specific sin, and the judgment is proportional, public, and reversible under one condition. The horror is that everyone on the floor watched him give the speech and said nothing. The spread targets deference, not proximity.

deployment_manifest.name: Lt. Mara Owen
deployment_manifest.physical_position: Point on the entry stack, eight meters from the corner office door
deployment_manifest.assigned_reason: Owen requested the assignment after reviewing the broadcast transcript; she has prior contact with a similar case and does not want a repeat outcome
deployment_manifest.visible_state: Moving with deliberate economy. Has not spoken since the elevator. Her hand keeps returning to the door-breach tool on her vest.

deployment_manifest.name: Brooks
deployment_manifest.mission_role: rear security, elevator and stairwell control
deployment_manifest.physical_position: Service elevator landing, monitoring stairwell B and the floor's secondary exit
deployment_manifest.assigned_reason: Standard rear-security assignment; Brooks knows the building layout from a prior unrelated call
deployment_manifest.visible_state: Calm on comms. Reported the carpet-pulling staff member before Owen did. Has not asked for clarification on the broadcast loop.

deployment_manifest.name: Specialist Park
deployment_manifest.mission_role: technical specialist, signal intercept and field medical
deployment_manifest.physical_position: Torah's left, two meters behind Owen, carrying field kit and portable jammer
deployment_manifest.assigned_reason: Park's jammer can interrupt the broadcast audio without destroying hardware, preserving the refutation option
deployment_manifest.visible_state: Has the jammer out but not armed. Watching the seated staff members more than the office door.

deployment_manifest.name: Dr. Saye
deployment_manifest.mission_role: remote analytical consult, broadcast and subject history
deployment_manifest.physical_position: Institute van, parking structure Level B2, on encrypted comms
deployment_manifest.assigned_reason: Saye pulled Voss's file and the broadcast transcript before deployment; she is the fastest path to a legally usable refutation document
deployment_manifest.visible_state: Her voice on comms is precise and slightly faster than usual. She has already drafted a one-paragraph withdrawal statement and is waiting to be told whether to transmit it.

rooms.root_sin: Self-exalting authority: the public declaration that one's own judgment supersedes all external accountability, made before witnesses who were given no standing to object
rooms.modern_incident_logic: Voss made the declaration in a sealed corporate environment where his authority was total and his subordinates were structurally prevented from dissent. The judgment activates in the space where the declaration was made and remains active as long as the declaration continues to broadcast in that space. The corporate floor is the throne room. The broadcast loop is the standing decree. The planter boxes are the only living things on the floor.
rooms.closure_test: The broadcast must be interrupted and the declaration formally refuted or withdrawn in the same physical space. A witness must be present. The refutation must be on record.
rooms.religious_pattern: Daniel 4: the king who built the great city by his own power is given an animal heart until seven times pass and he acknowledges that the highest authority grants dominion. The judgment is not permanent; it is proportional and reversible under one condition.
rooms.anomaly_rule: The degradation is active and spreading. It targets anyone who affirms or defers to the authority named in the declaration. It accelerates with each hour the broadcast runs. It does not respond to sedation, restraint, or destruction of the subject. It partially pauses if the broadcast audio is interrupted but does not reverse until the refutation is spoken or read aloud in the space.
rooms.forbidden_flat_read: Do not literalize as a werewolf or animal-possession scenario. The degradation is cognitive and postural, not a physical transformation into an animal. Voss looks wrong, moves wrong, and has lost language, but he is still recognizably human.
officer_reports: Owen, on approach: 'Two down in the corridor. Not injured. Not responding to name. Watch your footing near the planters — ceramic everywhere.'
Brooks, from the elevator: 'Stairwell B is clear to 36. Building security is holding at 35 and asking for a timeline. I told them we don't have one.'
Park, quietly: 'The jammer will kill the audio in this wing. It won't touch the server. If someone restarts the feed from downstairs, we're back to zero.'

Saye, on comms: 'Voss gave this speech at 08:31. It has been running for three hours and forty minutes. I have a withdrawal statement ready. Someone needs to read it on this floor, on record, before the seventh hour. That gives you three hours and twenty minutes.'
procedure_hooks: Broadcast intercept: Park's jammer can suppress audio without destroying hardware, buying time for the refutation
Evacuation of compromised staff before breach: field manual priority — clear civilians before engaging primary threat
Cordon of corner office: establish security on all glass panels before breach to prevent Voss exiting into the open floor
Evidence custody: Saye's withdrawal statement must be read aloud on the floor and recorded; the recording is the closure document
Triage of compromised staff post-closure: clinic referral for the two seated individuals showing early degradation signs

character_state_stakes.lt_mara_owen: Owen's prior contact case ended without closure. If this one closes cleanly, her fixation eases. If it fails or partially closes, she goes quiet in a specific way that the squad has learned to watch for.
character_state_stakes.specialist_mina_park: Park's jammer is her call. If she arms it too early and the refutation window closes, she carries that. If she holds it and someone gets hurt in the delay, she carries that differently.
character_state_stakes.brooks: Brooks is the only person on this mission who has not read the broadcast transcript. He is operating on secondhand information. If the situation requires him to make a judgment call without context, his response will be slower than Owen expects.
character_state_stakes.dr_lenora_saye: Saye drafted the withdrawal statement before anyone asked her to. If it is used and works, she will not mention it in the debrief. If it is not used, she will ask why, once, and then not ask again.

interlude_vectors: Owen does not debrief in the van. She walks the parking structure alone for four minutes before getting in.
Park checks the jammer's charge log after the mission. She does not explain why.
Saye's withdrawal statement, used or unused, ends up in the case file. Someone will read it later.

scenario_generation_contract.root_sin: Self-exalting authority: the public declaration that one's own judgment supersedes all external accountability, made before witnesses who were given no standing to object
scenario_generation_contract.modern_incident_logic: Voss made the declaration in a sealed corporate environment where his authority was total and his subordinates were structurally prevented from dissent. The judgment activates in the space where the declaration was made and remains active as long as the declaration continues to broadcast in that space. The corporate floor is the throne room. The broadcast loop is the standing decree. The planter boxes are the only living things on the floor.
scenario_generation_contract.closure_test: The broadcast must be interrupted and the declaration formally refuted or withdrawn in the same physical space. A witness must be present. The refutation must be on record.

religious_subtext.source_pattern: Daniel 4: the king who built the great city by his own power is given an animal heart until seven times pass and he acknowledges that the highest authority grants dominion. The judgment is not permanent; it is proportional and reversible under one condition.
religious_subtext.transformed_rule: The degradation is active and spreading. It targets anyone who affirms or defers to the authority named in the declaration. It accelerates with each hour the broadcast runs. It does not respond to sedation, restraint, or destruction of the subject. It partially pauses if the broadcast audio is interrupted but does not reverse until the refutation is spoken or read aloud in the space.
religious_subtext.forbidden_flat_read: Do not literalize as a werewolf or animal-possession scenario. The degradation is cognitive and postural, not a physical transformation into an animal. Voss looks wrong, moves wrong, and has lost language, but he is still recognizably human.

rooms.name: Devoted Property
rooms.description: A pursuit-class anomaly is routing personnel from a secured Institute storage facility. The cause is inside the building.
rooms.first_visit_description: Lt. Mara Owen has the exterior cordon. She is on comms from the parking structure across the street, coordinating the three injured contractors being triaged at the perimeter. Torah and Brooks are on the ground floor of the facility, past the first security door. Dr. Samira Iyad is remote—she pulled the chain-of-custody logs from Institute records twenty minutes ago and is reading them on a secure line. Agent Caleb Ross is present: he was already on-site when the manifestation started, which is why Owen flagged him. He is standing near the stairwell door to the lower level, not moving toward it. The lower corridor is audible from here—a low structural groan, rhythmic, like pressure building behind a wall that has no reason to flex. The emergency lighting is on. Two of the custody forms on the reception desk have been signed by Ross. One line on each form is blank where the item count should be.
rooms.return_description: The lower level is quieter now but not silent. The stairwell door is still warm to the touch. The reception desk forms are still there, the blank lines visible under the emergency lights.
rooms.detection_report: Institute logistics flagged a discrepancy in artifact transfer records from this facility three days ago. Before an audit could be scheduled, contractors reported a physical routing force in the lower corridor—described as a pressure wave that moved them bodily toward the exit. Two sustained soft-tissue injuries. A third lost consciousness briefly. Symbolic energy readings spiked to threshold on the Institute's passive monitoring grid at 0340 this morning.
rooms.current_situation: The pursuit-front is active in the lower corridor right now. Torah can hear it: a sustained low-frequency groan from the structure, and a directional pressure that makes the stairwell door rattle in its frame. Ross is standing six feet from that door with his arms crossed, not offering anything. Brooks is watching him. Iyad's voice comes through the earpiece: she has found a gap in the custody log—eleven items designated for Institute holding are unaccounted for, and the last person to sign the transfer sheet was Ross. Owen reports from the cordon that the pressure signature is expanding; it reached the lobby windows two minutes ago.

action_profile.physical_threat: The pursuit-front is a directed physical force: it moves through corridors as a pressure wave that can knock personnel off their feet, drive them into walls, and cause blunt trauma; it is currently contained to the lower level but expanding upward
action_profile.contact_state: The squad is under indirect contact—the pursuit-front has not yet reached the ground floor but is measurably advancing; anyone who enters the lower corridor is immediately in direct contact
action_profile.field_manual_drill: React to contact, establish security on the stairwell threshold, suppress/fix the advance of the pursuit-front by addressing its cause, evacuate injured contractors through the cordon, and break contact if the closure condition cannot be met before structural collapse
action_profile.tactical_objective: Hold the stairwell threshold, prevent the pursuit-front from reaching the ground floor and street cordon, locate and recover the hidden cache in the lower level, and complete the chain-of-custody transfer before the building envelope fails
action_profile.symbolic_close: Ross must verbally confess the theft by name—specific items, specific act—and the cache must be physically handed to a witnessed Institute officer; force alone cannot stop the pursuit-front because the cause is a moral debt, not a physical entity
action_profile.violent_resolution: The stairwell door can be sealed and braced to slow the pursuit-front's advance; the hidden storage unit in the lower level can be breached and the cache physically extracted under contact; the pursuit-front itself can be suppressed temporarily by establishing a security line at the threshold, buying time for the symbolic close
action_profile.not_monster_of_week: There is no creature. The pursuit-front is judgment made kinetic—it routes because the community is compromised by hidden greed, exactly as the Israelite force was routed at Ai. It cannot be killed. It can only be satisfied.

deployment_manifest.visible_state: Her voice is flat and precise on the earpiece; she reads numbers without editorial comment, which is its own kind of pressure

rooms.root_sin: Greed: Ross designated eleven recovered artifacts for Institute custody, then diverted them to a private storage unit and falsified the transfer count to cover the gap. The devoted property was never surrendered.
rooms.modern_incident_logic: The facility is a legitimate Institute secure-storage site. Ross had authorized access and used it to stage the diversion. The falsified forms are physically present on the reception desk. The hidden cache is in a locked secondary unit in the lower level, which is why the pursuit-front is centered there. The anomaly is not imported—it grew from the site of the original act.
rooms.closure_test: Ross must name the stolen items aloud in the presence of a witnessed Institute officer, the cache must be physically recovered from the lower level under contact, and the items must be transferred into Institute custody with a corrected chain-of-custody form signed on-site.
rooms.religious_pattern: Achan's sin: devoted property taken secretly, community routed in consequence, exposure through systematic inquiry, confession by name, recovery of hidden goods, restitution to the community.
rooms.anomaly_rule: The pursuit-front is directional and cause-linked. It advances toward the location of the hidden property and routes anyone between it and the exit. It cannot be destroyed. Its radius contracts when the confession is made and collapses when the cache is physically transferred. If the cache is moved without confession, the front follows it.
rooms.forbidden_flat_read: Do not literalize as a ghost, demon, or creature. The pursuit-front has no face, no voice, and no negotiable demands. It is pressure made from moral debt.
officer_reports: Brooks, ground floor: 'Ross was here before us. He knew where to stand.'
Owen, exterior cordon: 'Pressure signature just touched the lobby glass. We have maybe fifteen minutes before I have to pull the perimeter back.'
Iyad, remote: 'The last clean form is dated six weeks ago. After that, the item counts drop by one or two per sheet. Eleven total. All designated property. All signed by Ross.'
procedure_hooks: Chain-of-custody form review: blank item-count lines are visible evidence requiring a corrected entry
Stairwell threshold security: establish a hold line before anyone descends
Lower-level breach: the secondary storage unit requires physical entry under pursuit-front contact
Witnessed confession protocol: the Institute requires a named verbal statement in the presence of a senior officer for restitution to be valid
Contractor triage: Owen is managing three casualties at the perimeter; their status affects available personnel

character_state_stakes.lt_mara_owen: If the perimeter has to pull back, Owen carries that operationally; her composure is load-bearing for the cordon
character_state_stakes.brooks: Brooks has already read the situation; if Ross is protected or excused, Brooks's trust in command degrades visibly
character_state_stakes.dr_samira_iyad: Iyad's records work is the evidentiary spine; if it is dismissed or overridden, she withdraws from future operational support

rooms.name: Aldren Court — Security Level & Fourth Floor
rooms.description: A residential complex where every security camera has physically reoriented to a single window. Two workers are missing. The footage on every monitor shows a woman who should not be visible.
rooms.first_visit_description: Torah leads Owen and Brooks into the Aldren Court lobby at 0340. The building manager, Harlan Vess, was detained by local police an hour ago on an unrelated warrant; his assistant let the squad in and left immediately. Dr. Saye is remote, patched into the security office feed from Institute comms. The security office is on the ground floor: nine monitors, all showing the same image—apartment 4F window, 11:42 PM, fourteen months ago. The two missing maintenance workers, Dolan and Reyes, were last seen on the fourth-floor corridor at 2200. Owen has the floor plan and is running point on the fourth floor sweep. Brooks is on the security office, pulling the physical camera logs and the access history for 4F. Torah is the command node between them.
rooms.return_description: The monitors are dark now except one. It still shows 4F. The timestamp no longer loops—it advances, slowly, as if waiting for something to be said.
rooms.detection_report: Institute anomaly desk flagged the building at 0118 after a civilian 911 call described 'every TV in the lobby showing the same woman.' Local responders found no broadcast source. The security contractor confirmed the cameras had physically moved overnight. Two workers reported missing by a family member at 0300. Symbolic pressure index: elevated and rising.
rooms.current_situation: Owen's radio crackles from the fourth floor: she has eyes on the corridor and reports the cameras up there are moving—slow, deliberate, tracking her team's position. She cannot see Dolan or Reyes. Brooks is in the security office and has found a secondary hard drive hidden behind the main DVR rack, labeled with Vess's employee ID and a date range that covers the fourteen months in question. The footage on the monitors has not changed. The timestamp reads 11:42 and does not advance.

action_profile.physical_threat: The cameras on the fourth floor are physically tracking personnel; two workers are missing in the corridor and may be injured or pinned; the lenses are mounted on motorized pan-tilt hardware that can swing fast enough to strike at close range, and the corridor is narrow
action_profile.contact_state: Owen's fire team is in the fourth-floor corridor under active camera tracking; Brooks is isolated in the security office with the anomalous feed running on all nine monitors; the missing workers' location is unknown and the squad is split
action_profile.field_manual_drill: React to contact and establish security: Owen must fix the camera threat and clear the corridor room by room to locate the missing workers; Brooks must secure the evidence drive under field custody procedure before the feed can be used for disclosure
action_profile.tactical_objective: Locate and evacuate Dolan and Reyes from the fourth-floor corridor while Owen's team suppresses the camera tracking threat; simultaneously secure the hidden drive as evidence custody before it can be wiped remotely
action_profile.symbolic_close: A formal on-record naming of Vess, the victim, and the surveillance mechanism must be delivered—by Torah, using the recovered drive as the evidentiary basis—before the cameras release and the footage stops looping
action_profile.violent_resolution: The pan-tilt camera mounts on the fourth floor can be physically destroyed or disabled by Owen's team; this stops the tracking threat and frees the corridor but does not close the symbolic loop—the monitors in the security office will continue broadcasting until disclosure is made
action_profile.not_monster_of_week: The cameras are not possessed objects in a generic haunting sense; they are witnesses enacting a judgment logic derived from the Nathan parable—they will not stop pointing at the site of the original act until the act is named by someone with authority to put it on record; destroying them delays the physical danger but leaves the symbolic pressure unresolved and escalating to the next available surface

rooms.root_sin: A building manager used his institutional access to the security infrastructure—cameras, master keys, maintenance schedules—to surveil a tenant he desired, arrange for her private moments to be recorded without consent, and then use that footage as leverage. When she reported it internally, he routed the complaint back to himself and had her lease terminated. The surveillance was the weapon; the concealment was the second crime; the erasure of her complaint was the third.
rooms.modern_incident_logic: Vess installed a secondary DVR on a hidden drive using his own employee credentials, set cameras to record apartment 4F on a schedule tied to the tenant's known routine, and suppressed her formal complaint through his administrative access. The symbolic pressure accumulated at the site of the original act—the window, the camera angle, the timestamp—and when Vess was removed from the building by police, the suppression mechanism collapsed. The cameras, no longer directed by the person who weaponized them, defaulted to the only instruction still encoded in the symbolic record: witness.
rooms.closure_test: Brooks must secure the hidden drive under evidence custody. Owen must clear the fourth floor and recover Dolan and Reyes. Torah must then deliver a formal on-record naming—spoken aloud into the Institute log with the drive cited as evidence—identifying Vess, the victim by name if known, and the mechanism of surveillance. The cameras release when the naming is complete.
rooms.religious_pattern: Nathan parable structure: the sin is committed by a powerful figure using institutional position; the evidence is hidden; a witness arrives and names the act directly to the one with authority to record it; the naming is the judgment and the closure simultaneously
rooms.anomaly_rule: Witness-class manifestation: the infrastructure of the original sin becomes a compulsive witness, orienting toward the site of the act and looping the moment of exposure. It tracks anyone who enters the sightline because the original act was about being seen without consent—the anomaly inverts this, making the watcher watched. It cannot be closed by destruction alone because a witness silenced is not a witness satisfied.
rooms.forbidden_flat_read: Do not literalize as a ghost of the victim or a demonic possession of the cameras; the cameras are not haunted, they are enacting a judgment logic that requires testimony, not exorcism
officer_reports: Owen, radio: 'Camera on the east end just tracked Specialist Park through a full ninety-degree arc. It's not on a motion sensor. It followed her.'
Brooks, security office: 'This drive was formatted with Vess's credentials. The date range starts six weeks before the cameras were officially installed. He was recording before the system was live.'

Dr. Saye, comms: 'The footage timestamp doesn't advance. In every case I've documented with this pattern, the loop holds until someone with authority says what happened out loud and puts it somewhere it can't be deleted.'
procedure_hooks: Evidence custody: Brooks must photograph the drive in situ, log its location relative to the DVR rack, and bag it before removal—standard chain-of-custody; if skipped, the drive's evidentiary value is compromised and the disclosure may not satisfy the symbolic closure condition

Corridor sweep: Owen's team uses a two-element bounding movement to clear each doorway on the fourth floor, with one element fixed on the camera arc while the other moves—standard react-to-contact drill adapted for a non-ballistic tracking threat

Formal disclosure: Torah's naming must be delivered into the Institute's live mission log, not informally; the log entry is the record that cannot be deleted, which is the structural requirement the anomaly is enforcing

character_state_stakes.lt_mara_owen: If the corridor sweep goes wrong and Dolan or Reyes is found injured, Owen carries the weight of the split-force decision; her composure is load-bearing for the rest of the squad
character_state_stakes.brooks: Brooks found the drive; if the evidence chain is broken on his watch, he knows it and it shows in how he handles the next mission's paperwork
character_state_stakes.dr_lenora_saye: Saye identified the closure condition before anyone else; if Torah acts on her read and it works, her standing with the squad improves; if it fails, she goes quiet in a way that is worse than argument
character_state_stakes.torah: Torah must say the name on record; the act of naming is the player's moral commitment, not just a mechanical close

rooms.name: Shaft Seven, Reiss Incident
rooms.description: A utility shaft at an Institute-adjacent infrastructure site. A technician is trapped below. The gantry is failing. The paperwork says it was an accident.
rooms.first_visit_description: Torah leads Owen and Brooks to the Dothan Industrial Annex at 0340. The site is a decommissioned water-treatment complex the Institute uses for deep-equipment storage. Shaft Seven is a 40-meter vertical access bore, dry, concrete-lined. Owen is on the gantry level with a rope kit and a radio. Brooks is at the site entrance holding the incident file, which he flagged as inconsistent during transit. Dr. Saye is remote, patched in by comms from the Institute clinic, monitoring Reiss's vitals via the shaft's emergency sensor package. Three of Reiss's colleagues are present on-site: they have not moved from the break room since filing the malfunction report eleven hours ago. The gantry groans every forty seconds. From below, a voice rises in regular intervals, not screaming, just speaking, and each time it does the concrete around the shaft lip fractures another centimeter outward.
rooms.return_description: The shaft is quieter now. The gantry has been secured or is gone. The break room is empty. Whatever was filed here will follow the squad.
rooms.detection_report: Institute seismic array flagged rhythmic sub-surface pressure events at the Dothan Annex at 0110. Civilian emergency services were turned away at 0200 by an on-site supervisor citing an active Institute protocol. The supervisor's name appears on the malfunction report.
rooms.current_situation: Owen is already clipped to the gantry rail, looking down. She radios: the shaft floor is visible, Reiss is standing, not injured, but he cannot climb. Every time he tries, the shaft wall pushes him back down. The gantry shudders. A section of the access ladder has already separated and fallen. Brooks holds up the incident file: three signatures, one timestamp that predates the reported malfunction by six hours. The colleagues in the break room have not come out.

action_profile.physical_threat: The failing gantry can drop a section onto Owen or anyone on the rope line at any moment; the pressure pulses from the shaft transmit through the metal frame as concussive shockwaves that can knock personnel off the platform
action_profile.contact_state: Owen is on the gantry under active structural failure; the squad is in contact with the anomaly the moment they step onto the platform
action_profile.field_manual_drill: React to contact on the gantry: suppress the structural failure by establishing a secondary anchor point, fix Reiss's position with a rope line, bound the extraction team in two-person relay, breach the false-report chain to satisfy the symbolic close condition
action_profile.tactical_objective: Extract Reiss from the shaft before the gantry fails completely, while the shaft's symbolic pressure is still active
action_profile.symbolic_close: The false incident report must be formally repudiated on record by a signatory; force alone cannot stop the shaft's pressure pulses
action_profile.violent_resolution: The gantry itself can be partially demolished in a controlled drop to relieve load on the anchor points, buying extraction time at the cost of the primary egress route
action_profile.not_monster_of_week: There is no creature. The shaft is a judgment engine powered by collective envy and institutional silence. The threat is structural, procedural, and moral simultaneously. The colleagues eating in the break room while Reiss was below is the horror.

deployment_manifest.visible_state: Her voice on comms is steady but she asks for a pulse update every three minutes, which is more often than the sensors require.

rooms.root_sin: Collective envy of a favored colleague, expressed as deliberate disposal and covered by fabricated institutional paperwork
rooms.modern_incident_logic: Reiss was the only technician on the team to receive a commendation and a project lead assignment last quarter. His colleagues filed a false equipment-malfunction report, lured him to Shaft Seven on a pretext, and left him below. The shaft's symbolic resonance activates because the act mirrors the source pattern exactly: the favored one stripped of status, dropped into a dry pit, while the group returns to ordinary work. The Institute's own documentation system becomes the coat: the false report is the physical evidence of the original sin, and it replicates because the sin has not been named.
rooms.closure_test: Reiss extracted alive AND at least one signatory of the false report formally repudiates it on record in the presence of a witness before the squad leaves the site
rooms.religious_pattern: Joseph pit narrative: favored marker, envious brothers, dry pit, false evidence, eating while the victim calls below, transit vector as escape route for the perpetrators
rooms.anomaly_rule: The shaft exerts symbolic gravity on favored-marker objects and persons; it amplifies the trapped person's voice as concussive pressure; it resists extraction until the false record is broken; it deepens if the squad leaves without closure
rooms.forbidden_flat_read: Do not literalize as a monster in the shaft or a supernatural entity controlling the colleagues; the horror is human envy made structurally consequential
officer_reports: Owen, radio: 'Gantry section three is gone. I have eyes on Reiss. He's standing. He's talking but I can't make out words. Every time he talks the frame moves.'
Brooks, at entrance: 'This report was filed at 1800 yesterday. The malfunction it describes happened at 2200. Someone wrote the ending before the event.'
Saye, comms: 'Reiss's vitals are stable but his cortisol is flat. That's not fear. That's resignation. He stopped expecting rescue hours ago.'
procedure_hooks: Rope-rescue relay: two-person anchor-and-lower, one person on the line, one person on the brake
Incident report authentication: Institute protocol requires a witness signature and a timestamp audit to formally void a filed report
Gantry load assessment: controlled demolition of a failing section to redistribute structural load is a recognized field procedure
Evidence custody: the original false report must be physically retained and logged, not destroyed, for the repudiation to be valid

character_state_stakes.lt_mara_owen: If the gantry drops while she is on it, Owen takes injury and stress; if extraction succeeds under her lead, her squad cohesion improves
character_state_stakes.brooks: If Brooks forces the repudiation and it holds, his institute standing increases; if he is ignored or the report replicates before he acts, he carries a fixation state
character_state_stakes.dr_lenora_saye: If Reiss is extracted alive, Saye's remote-consult confidence stabilizes; if Reiss dies while she is monitoring vitals, she enters a withdrawn state

rooms.name: Harlan Processing Facility — Floor 3
rooms.description: A mid-shift industrial floor. Workers down. No attacker on camera. Laceration pattern consistent across all casualties. The clock is running.
rooms.first_visit_description: Torah leads Ross and Owen through the stairwell door onto Floor 3 of the Harlan Processing Facility at 14:22. The shift was mid-cycle when the calls came in: six workers down in eleven minutes, no intruder on any camera feed, no alarm triggered. Dr. Saye is remote, patched into Torah's comms from the Institute's medical bay with the casualty files already open. Brooks is holding the ground-floor cordon with two facility security officers. The floor smells of machine oil and something older, copper-heavy, that does not belong here. Three bodies are visible from the stairwell door. A fourth worker is crouched behind a press unit, alive, not moving, staring at the center aisle. The overhead lights are steady. Nothing is moving. Then the sound begins: a low rhythmic impact, like a blade finding a sheath, repeating every few seconds from somewhere past the press line.
rooms.return_description: Floor 3 is quieter now. The press units are still. The center aisle holds the evidence of what happened here, and the facility's HR archive terminal is still open on the supervisor's desk, the grievance file Torah pulled still on screen.
rooms.detection_report: Facility security flagged six simultaneous medical emergencies at 14:11. Responding paramedics reported laceration injuries inconsistent with any machinery on the floor. No blood trail. No weapon recovered. Institute monitoring flagged a wrath-class symbolic pressure spike at 14:09, two minutes before the first body dropped.
rooms.current_situation: Torah, Ross, and Owen are inside the kill zone. The echo is active in the center aisle, invisible but audible: that repeating impact sound is moving. The fourth worker is still alive behind the press unit. Dr. Saye is calling casualty status over comms. Brooks is on the ground floor and cannot reach the squad without breaching the stairwell door, which would expose the cordon. The echo has not yet targeted the squad. It will.

action_profile.physical_threat: The wrath-echo produces sword-pattern lacerations on anyone it reaches; it moves through the center aisle and can injure or kill personnel on contact; it does not distinguish between guilty and bystander
action_profile.contact_state: The squad is inside the active kill zone; the echo is audible and closing; they have seconds before it completes its current pass and reorients
action_profile.field_manual_drill: React to contact: personnel in the kill zone return suppressive action on the echo's position while non-contact personnel fix the survivor and establish a secondary position; bounding overwatch to extract the survivor; symbolic close requires holding position under pressure
action_profile.tactical_objective: Suppress the echo long enough to extract the surviving worker, then locate and read the original grievance file to name the excess and close the phenomenon
action_profile.symbolic_close: The grievance file in the facility HR archive must be read aloud on-site; the reader must explicitly name the disproportionate scope as excess, not as justice; the echo cannot be permanently destroyed by force alone
action_profile.violent_resolution: The echo has a physical anchor: a supervisor's locked cabinet on the center aisle contains a set of industrial cutting tools that were used in a documented workplace assault three years prior and never formally processed; destroying or sealing the cabinet suppresses the echo's movement range and reduces its lethality, buying time for the symbolic close
action_profile.not_monster_of_week: The echo is not a creature; it is the unresolved shape of collective punishment repeating itself; it targets by proximity and indifference, exactly as the original act did; fighting it without confession only mirrors the original sin

rooms.root_sin: A workplace assault three years prior was answered by the victim's two brothers with a coordinated attack on the entire night shift, injuring four people who had no involvement; the brothers documented their grievance in an HR complaint but the complaint was closed without acknowledgment of the excess; neither brother was ever asked to account for the scope, only the original wrong
rooms.modern_incident_logic: The facility is the site of the original assault and the retaliatory attack; the HR archive still holds the closed grievance file; the cutting tools used in the retaliation were confiscated and locked in a supervisor's cabinet but never formally processed or destroyed; the wrath-echo is anchored to the unprocessed tools and the unread file, both physically present on Floor 3
rooms.closure_test: Locate the HR grievance file, read it aloud on-site, and explicitly name the retaliatory scope as disproportionate; the echo requires the excess to be spoken, not just felt
rooms.religious_pattern: Simeon and Levi: justified grievance, collective punishment, plunder of the dead, no accounting for the excess; Jonah's anger at mercy: the refusal to accept that restraint is also an answer
rooms.anomaly_rule: The echo moves through the center aisle on a repeating pass; it cannot be permanently stopped by force; destroying or sealing the tool cabinet reduces its range and speed; reading the grievance file and naming the excess extinguishes it; if the squad mirrors the original act by using disproportionate force without the symbolic close, the echo intensifies
rooms.forbidden_flat_read: Do not literalize as a ghost of the brothers or a demon; the echo is the shape of the act itself, not a person
officer_reports: Ross, on comms: 'Saye, I'm counting the interval. It's not random. It's moving like it has a route.'
Owen, low: 'Cabinet on the aisle is padlocked. That wasn't in the facility layout.'
Saye, remote: 'Laceration depth is consistent across all six. Whatever this is, it doesn't vary its force. That's not rage. That's method.'
Brooks, ground floor: 'Nobody else comes in. I don't care what they say their badge level is.'
procedure_hooks: React-to-contact drill: suppress echo movement, fix survivor position, bound to extraction point
Evidence custody: the HR grievance file is a document in an active facility archive; reading it aloud constitutes a formal acknowledgment under Institute protocol
Tool cabinet: sealed evidence item requiring forced entry or key recovery from the facility supervisor's body on the floor
Casualty triage: Saye needs Ross to relay wound measurements from two bodies to confirm the echo's targeting pattern before the symbolic close

character_state_stakes.dr_lenora_saye: If Saye's casualty analysis correctly identifies the targeting pattern and Torah acts on it, Saye's confidence in remote consult work increases; if the squad ignores her data and takes casualties, she goes silent in the debrief
character_state_stakes.torah: If Torah reads the grievance file and names the excess, the echo closes and Torah carries the weight of having spoken someone else's unconfessed sin; if Torah refuses or delegates, the echo does not close

rooms.name: Morrow Street Relief Kitchen
rooms.description: A city relief kitchen. Cold-store door under pressure. Fourteen people eating without stopping.
rooms.first_visit_description: Torah leads Ross and Owen through the loading bay. Three dead birds—small, gray, no visible entry wound—are arranged along the bay threshold as if they landed and did not rise. The smell is warm protein, not rot. Dr. Iyad is already inside via the clinic entrance, triaging the seated patients. Brooks is on the roof with a radio line to Institute command. The kitchen director, Harlan Voss, is not present; his office is locked. The distribution log on the service counter shows forty-one recipients for today's lunch run. The sign-in sheet at the door shows one hundred and nine.
rooms.return_description: The loading bay smells different now—closer to nothing. The birds are gone. Someone has propped the cold-store door open with a fire extinguisher. The canteen chairs are still pushed back at odd angles.
rooms.detection_report: Institute field sensor flagged anomalous thermal signature in the cold-store at 14:22—contents registering body temperature despite refrigeration. Clinic staff called emergency services at 14:31 when patients could not be redirected from eating. First responders withdrew at 14:44 after two EMTs began exhibiting the same seated behavior.
rooms.current_situation: The cold-store door is visibly bowing. Fourteen people sit at canteen tables in a ring, lifting food to their mouths in slow rotation. The food in their hands—bread, rice, packaged meals—does not decrease. Dr. Iyad is crouched beside the nearest patient, gloved, not touching the food. She looks up when Torah enters and says nothing for two full seconds.

action_profile.physical_threat: The cold-store door can fail at any time, releasing pressurized contents—dense, warm, semi-liquid surplus—into the canteen at force sufficient to knock personnel down and coat exposed skin; contact with the discharge triggers swallow-lock within ninety seconds. The seated patients are not violent but will physically resist removal, and their grip strength is abnormal.
action_profile.contact_state: Squad is inside the building with the anomaly already active. The cold-store is fifteen meters from their position. The seated group occupies the direct path between the squad and the store.
action_profile.field_manual_drill: React to contact: establish security perimeter around the cold-store door; suppress/fix the seated group by isolating them from additional food sources; breach the director's office for the distribution log; evacuate non-affected kitchen staff through the loading bay.
action_profile.tactical_objective: Prevent cold-store door failure, extract or isolate the fourteen seated patients, and secure the distribution log before Voss or anyone else can remove it.
action_profile.symbolic_close: The hoarded surplus must leave the building outward—distributed to the queue still waiting outside or destroyed under witnessed record. The log must be corrected. Until that happens, new victims continue to join the seated group regardless of how many are extracted.
action_profile.violent_resolution: The cold-store door and its frame can be breached outward with the loading bay forklift, venting the pressurized contents into the open bay rather than the canteen—this stops the door-failure threat and buys time, but the discharge in the open bay is still hazardous and must be contained or distributed before it re-pressurizes in the drain system.
action_profile.not_monster_of_week: There is no creature. The threat is provision itself—food that was meant to move and was stopped. The violence is the surplus asserting its original purpose by force. The squad is not fighting a monster; they are inside a judgment that is still in progress.

deployment_manifest.assigned_reason: Clinical assessment of swallow-lock presentation; prior anomalous ingestion casework
deployment_manifest.visible_state: Gloved, crouched, watching the patient's throat. Her kit is open but nothing is drawn.

deployment_manifest.name: Brooks
deployment_manifest.physical_position: Roof, radio line to command
deployment_manifest.assigned_reason: Keeps the perimeter clear and manages external pressure so the squad can work inside
deployment_manifest.visible_state: Voice only. Calm. Reporting vehicle count outside every three minutes.

rooms.root_sin: Systematic hoarding of donated food relief—diverting surplus beyond daily provision into private storage while registered recipients went without, treating communal provision as personal inventory.
rooms.modern_incident_logic: The kitchen director falsified recipient counts over eleven weeks, accumulating a cold-store surplus that was never meant to sit. The Institute's symbolic threshold model predicts that withheld provision under a distribution covenant—food donated specifically for redistribution—accumulates symbolic pressure proportional to the gap between what was given and what was passed on. At critical mass, the surplus stops being inert. The kitchen is a modern distribution covenant site; the hoarding is a modern craving-beyond-portion. The plague follows the same logic it always did.
rooms.closure_test: Confirm the gap between the sign-in sheet and the distribution log; locate and open the cold-store; redistribute or destroy the surplus under witnessed record; correct the log under Owen's custody. Swallow-lock in patients resolves when the surplus is no longer contained.
rooms.religious_pattern: Numbers 11 quail plague: craving beyond provision, gathering beyond the day's portion, judgment arriving before the food is consumed. Exodus 16 manna rule: what is kept beyond the morning becomes foul. The two sources combine—hoarding is the sin, the food itself becomes the instrument of judgment.
rooms.anomaly_rule: The surplus is not spoiled; it is activated. It exerts pressure proportional to how long it has been withheld. Contact with discharged surplus transfers the craving-lock. The lock resolves when the surplus moves outward. Destruction counts as distribution if witnessed and recorded.
rooms.forbidden_flat_read: Do not literalize as food poisoning, bacterial contamination, or a supernatural entity living in the food. The food is not possessed. It is provision that has been denied its purpose and is asserting it.
officer_reports: Owen: 'Log shows forty-one served. Door count says one-oh-nine. That gap is eleven weeks deep—I can see the correction fluid on the earlier pages.'
Iyad: 'They are chewing. Continuously. The food in their hands is not going down and it is not going away. I have no clinical category for this.'
Brooks via radio: 'Voss's car is in the lot. He did not go home. He is somewhere in the building.'
Ross: 'Cold-store door is warm to the back of the hand from here. Whatever is in there is not cold.'
procedure_hooks: Evidence chain of custody: Owen photographs and bags the distribution log before any intervention
Breach protocol: forklift in loading bay can be used to vent the cold-store outward
Triage isolation: seated patients must be separated from additional food sources before extraction
Perimeter: Brooks manages external responders and press; squad has a closing window before the scene goes public
Witness requirement: redistribution or destruction of surplus must be logged and signed to satisfy closure condition

character_state_stakes.agent_caleb_ross: If he enters the canteen without a clear plan and a patient grips him, he risks swallow-lock contact; failure raises his injury and contamination state
character_state_stakes.lt_mara_owen: If the log is removed or destroyed before she secures it, her evidence chain fails and Institute scrutiny increases; success anchors her as the accountability officer for this case
character_state_stakes.dr_samira_iyad: Extended proximity to the seated group without resolution degrades her clinical confidence; if she attempts a medical intervention that fails visibly, her stress state rises and she becomes fixated on the patients rather than the store
character_state_stakes.torah: Symbolic asset; if Torah is present at the redistribution moment, closure probability increases; if Torah delays, the cold-store door fails on a timer

interlude_vectors: Owen keeps the corrected log. She will reference it in a future evidence review if the director is ever located.
Iyad's patient notes from the seated group become an anomalous ingestion case file—a future mission hook if the swallow-lock reappears elsewhere.
Ross does not talk about the grip strength. He mentions it once in the debrief and then does not mention it again.

rooms.name: Station Kaph — Threshold Watch
rooms.description: A sealed Institute watch station. The blast door is closing. Two personnel are inside. The watch officer falsified eleven weeks of logs.
rooms.first_visit_description: Torah leads the forward element. Lt. Mara Owen is on point at the blast door with Brooks providing physical suppression support. Specialist Mina Park is at the external terminal, remote-jacked into what remains of the station's comms. Dr. Lenora Saye is on remote consult from Institute command—she pulled the original anomaly flag that Holt buried and is the one who called the deployment. Agent Caleb Ross is inside the station; he was conducting a routine audit when the door began cycling and is now trapped with technician Vasek. Holt is unaccounted for—his duty chair is empty, his log terminal still warm.
rooms.return_description: The blast door has moved another increment. The ant-pattern noise on the comms is louder. Ross's last transmission was clipped mid-sentence.
rooms.detection_report: Institute remote monitoring flagged Station Kaph at 0340 when the blast door began an unsanctioned closure cycle. Comms degraded within six minutes. Dr. Saye cross-referenced the anomaly log and found eleven weeks of suppressed sensor reports—all signed by Watch Officer Holt, all marked filed, none transmitted. Two personnel confirmed inside by last headcount.
rooms.current_situation: The blast door is at the third increment of closure—roughly 60 centimeters narrower than standard. A low, rhythmic grinding comes from the door mechanism, not mechanical: it pulses in irregular bursts, like something coordinating without a center. The external terminal shows ant-pattern signal interference crawling across every sensor channel in the same direction, inward. On the duty desk visible through the narrowing gap: a stack of paper logs, hand-annotated, rubber-banded. Holt's chair faces away from the door. The readiness board behind it shows every shift marked green. Ross's voice comes through Park's relay in fragments—he has found the buried report. He cannot transmit it out. The door moves again.

action_profile.physical_threat: The blast door's incremental closure can crush personnel attempting to pass through or hold it. Each increment is slow but irreversible by mechanical means. If Ross or Vasek attempt to exit without the door being held, they risk being caught in the gap. The ant-pattern signal interference is actively degrading Park's terminal link—if it completes, external comms and door override are lost entirely.
action_profile.contact_state: The squad is under time-compression contact: the door is moving now, comms are degrading now, and Ross is inside with a document the squad needs. Owen is physically bracing against the door frame. Brooks is on suppression of the terminal interference. The threat is not waiting.
action_profile.field_manual_drill: React to contact and establish security: Owen holds the door gap while Brooks suppresses the signal interference at the terminal. Bound forward: send one element through the gap to reach Ross and recover the buried report. Breach and clear the duty station. Evacuate Ross and Vasek through the held gap. Break contact once the report is filed and the door cycle releases.
action_profile.tactical_objective: Hold the door gap open long enough to get Ross and the buried readiness report out, file the report through Dr. Saye's remote chain, and evacuate all personnel before the final increment seals the station.
action_profile.symbolic_close: The buried readiness report must be formally filed—read aloud to Dr. Saye over the relay and logged into the Institute's reporting chain—before the door cycle will release. Force can hold the gap but cannot reverse the increments. Accountability is the only key.
action_profile.violent_resolution: The blast door mechanism can be physically engaged: Brooks can drive a breaching tool into the door track to arrest one increment, buying time. This is loud, dangerous, and the door will resume cycling after roughly eight minutes. It is a delay, not a solution. The ant-pattern interference node at the terminal can be physically destroyed by Park, cutting the signal degradation—but this also cuts the remote filing link to Dr. Saye, making symbolic closure harder.
action_profile.not_monster_of_week: There is no creature. The door is the judgment. It moves because the watch was not kept—each increment is a missed calibration, a buried report, a falsified green light. The squad is not fighting a monster; they are racing the consequence of someone else's sloth, and the only way to stop it is to do the work that was never done.

deployment_manifest.name: Lt. Mara Owen
deployment_manifest.physical_position: At the blast door exterior, physically bracing the gap
deployment_manifest.assigned_reason: Senior field officer; Owen ran the original Station Kaph readiness audit two months ago and flagged irregularities that were overruled by Holt's paperwork
deployment_manifest.visible_state: Controlled, jaw set, not speaking unless spoken to. She recognized Holt's handwriting on the log stack through the gap.

deployment_manifest.name: Brooks
deployment_manifest.physical_position: At the external terminal beside Park, monitoring the signal interference
deployment_manifest.assigned_reason: Physical suppression and breach capability; if the door needs to be forced, Brooks is the option
deployment_manifest.visible_state: Restless. He keeps looking at the door gap instead of the terminal. He wants to go in.

deployment_manifest.name: Specialist Mina Park
deployment_manifest.physical_position: External terminal, remote-jacked into station comms
deployment_manifest.assigned_reason: Only squad member with the clearance and skill to interface with Kaph's sealed terminal architecture
deployment_manifest.visible_state: Focused but tense. The interference is eating her connection in real time and she knows it.

deployment_manifest.name: Dr. Lenora Saye
deployment_manifest.physical_position: Institute command, remote relay
deployment_manifest.assigned_reason: She pulled the original buried anomaly flag and has the authority to formally receive and log the readiness report
deployment_manifest.visible_state: Precise and quiet on the relay. She has already drafted the incident form. She is waiting for someone to read her the report.

deployment_manifest.name: Agent Caleb Ross
deployment_manifest.physical_position: Inside Station Kaph, at Holt's duty station
deployment_manifest.assigned_reason: Was conducting a routine audit when the door began cycling; now the only person who can physically reach the buried report
deployment_manifest.visible_state: Transmitting in fragments. His voice is steady. He has the report in his hands.

rooms.root_sin: Watch Officer Holt falsified eleven weeks of readiness logs, buried anomaly sensor reports, and slept through every calibration window. The station's threshold was left unguarded. The door is closing because no one kept the watch.
rooms.modern_incident_logic: Station Kaph is a real Institute monitoring post with a blast door rated for symbolic containment events. The door's closure protocol was designed to activate if the station's anomaly threshold was breached without a filed readiness report on record. Holt's falsified logs created the appearance of compliance while the actual threshold went unmonitored. When the anomaly finally crossed the threshold, the protocol triggered—but found no valid readiness report to halt against. The door began closing on its own logic, and the ant-pattern interference is the station's sensor grid executing the last eleven weeks of unfiled calibration tasks simultaneously, inward, without direction.
rooms.closure_test: Recover the buried readiness report from inside the station, evacuate Ross and Vasek, and file the report through Dr. Saye's remote chain before the door seals. The door cycle will release only after the report is formally logged.
rooms.religious_pattern: Sluggard/ant: the ant works without overseer; the sluggard sleeps and loses the harvest. Watchfulness: the master comes at an unknown hour; the unprepared house is broken into. Buried talent: the servant who buried his talent is cast out. The door shuts on the foolish who were not ready.
rooms.anomaly_rule: The door advances one increment per failed plan. At five increments it seals permanently. Force can arrest one increment per use of the breaching option but cannot reverse the count. Filing the report halts the cycle entirely and begins a slow reversal.
rooms.forbidden_flat_read: Do not literalize the ants as a creature swarm. The ant-pattern is a signal behavior—coordinated, leaderless, purposeful—not a biological infestation.
officer_reports: Owen, at the gap: 'The logs on that desk are hand-written. Every shift, green. Holt's initials. Eleven weeks.'
Park, at the terminal: 'The interference is moving inward on every channel at the same rate. It's not random. It's finishing something.'
Ross, fragmented relay: '—found the report. Sensor anomaly, week three. He knew. He—' [static] '—buried under the duty desk. I have it.'
Saye, remote: 'I have the incident form open. Someone needs to read me that report. Word for word. That is the procedure.'
procedure_hooks: Blast door breach: breaching tool into door track arrests one increment, buys eight minutes
Signal interference suppression: destroying the interference node cuts degradation but also cuts the remote filing link
Evidence recovery: Ross must physically hand the report through the gap or transmit it page by page
Formal filing: Saye must receive the report read aloud over the relay and confirm log entry—this is the closure procedure
Evacuation: Ross and Vasek must exit through the held gap before the final increment

character_state_stakes.lt_mara_owen: If Owen holds the door and the plan succeeds, her earlier overruled audit flag is vindicated—loyalty and morale stabilize. If she is injured in the gap, she carries a physical reminder of Holt's negligence.
character_state_stakes.brooks: If Brooks uses the breaching option and it buys the time needed, his confidence holds. If the door resumes and catches someone, he fractures—he was the one who said he could hold it.
character_state_stakes.specialist_mina_park: If Park preserves the comms link and filing succeeds, she is steady. If she destroys the interference node and cuts Saye's link, she has to live with having made closure harder.
character_state_stakes.agent_caleb_ross: If Ross gets out with the report, he is shaken but functional. If the door seals with him inside, he becomes an unresolved remainder—alive in a closed space, unreachable.
character_state_stakes.dr_lenora_saye: If the report is filed, Saye's original flag is on record as correct. If closure fails, she carries the knowledge that she was right and was overruled.

interlude_vectors: Post-mission: Owen sits with the rubber-banded log stack. She does not open it again.
Cooldown: Brooks does not talk about the door. He talks about something else entirely.
Follow-up: Holt's location is still unresolved. The station is open but the duty chair is still facing away.
